8.1.subsection: Responsibilities of the Board of Directors
8.1.title: Board Approval of Technology Risk Appetite

business.summary: Without active board oversight of technology risk, the institution faces uncontrolled exposure to cyber threats, system failures, and regulatory penalties that can erode customer trust and shareholder value.

requirements.requirement: Allocate a dedicated annual budget for technology risk management activities, including cybersecurity tools, skilled personnel, and third-party assessments, with board-level approval and quarterly spend tracking
requirements.rationale: Underfunding technology risk leaves the institution vulnerable to breaches and outages that cost far more than prevention; BNM expects visible resource commitment as evidence of board seriousness

requirements.requirement: Integrate technology risk as a standing agenda item in board meetings with dedicated discussion time, not relegated to Any Other Business
requirements.rationale: Technology risk that is buried under general items receives insufficient board attention, leading to uninformed decision-making on matters that can disrupt the entire institution

requirements.requirement: Ensure technology risk is formally embedded within the enterprise risk management framework with clear linkages to credit, market, and operational risk categories
requirements.rationale: Treating technology risk in isolation creates blind spots; interconnected risks such as cyber-enabled fraud or system outages affecting trading platforms require an integrated view for effective board oversight

technology.summary: IT teams must provide the board with accurate, timely data on the institution's technology risk posture through dashboards, metrics, and reporting systems that translate technical risks into business impact language.

requirements.requirement: Implement a technology risk dashboard or reporting platform that aggregates key risk indicators across infrastructure, applications, and cybersecurity domains for board-level consumption
requirements.rationale: The board cannot exercise effective oversight without consolidated, accurate, and current data on the institution's technology risk landscape presented in business-relevant terms

requirements.requirement: Maintain a comprehensive technology asset inventory that underpins risk assessments and enables the board to understand the scope and criticality of technology dependencies
requirements.rationale: You cannot manage risks to assets you do not know exist; an accurate inventory is the foundation for all technology risk reporting to the board

requirements.requirement: Establish automated monitoring and alerting for critical technology risk indicators such as system availability, security incidents, and patch compliance rates
requirements.rationale: Manual monitoring is insufficient for the speed and volume of modern technology threats; automated systems ensure the board receives timely and accurate risk data

governance.summary: Formal governance structures must ensure the board has clear authority, defined responsibilities, and documented processes for overseeing technology risk across the institution.

requirements.requirement: Establish and maintain a Board Technology Risk Charter that defines the board's roles, responsibilities, and authority in overseeing technology risk management
requirements.rationale: Without a formal charter, accountability for technology risk oversight becomes ambiguous, and BNM examiners will question whether the board takes its oversight obligations seriously

requirements.requirement: Document and enforce a formal escalation framework that ensures material technology risk events are reported to the board within defined timeframes
requirements.rationale: Delayed escalation of critical technology incidents can result in regulatory breaches and reputational damage; the board must be informed promptly to fulfil its oversight role

requirements.requirement: Require board sign-off on the technology risk management framework, risk appetite statement, and any material changes to the institution's technology risk profile
requirements.rationale: Board approval creates accountability and ensures alignment between strategic direction and technology risk tolerance, which is a core BNM expectation under RMiT

8.2.subsection: Responsibilities of the Board of Directors
8.2.title: Board Oversight Responsibilities

business.summary: A dedicated board-level technology risk committee ensures focused oversight of technology threats that could disrupt banking operations, compromise customer data, or trigger regulatory sanctions.

requirements.requirement: Establish a board-level technology risk committee with a formal charter that defines its scope, authority, and relationship to other board committees such as the Board Risk Committee and Audit Committee
requirements.rationale: Without a dedicated committee, technology risk competes for attention with credit, market, and other risks, leading to insufficient scrutiny of an area that increasingly drives operational and reputational exposure

requirements.requirement: Ensure the committee meets at least quarterly with documented agendas covering cybersecurity posture, technology incidents, risk appetite adherence, and emerging technology threats
requirements.rationale: Quarterly meetings are the BNM-mandated minimum; less frequent meetings leave gaps in board awareness during periods when the threat landscape evolves rapidly

requirements.requirement: Allocate budget for external technology and cybersecurity advisors to supplement committee expertise where internal knowledge gaps exist
requirements.rationale: Malaysian banking institutions may not always have board members with deep technical expertise; external advisors ensure the committee can effectively challenge management on complex technology risk matters

technology.summary: Technology teams must provide the committee with structured reporting, data-driven insights, and access to subject matter experts to enable informed oversight decisions.

requirements.requirement: Develop standardised committee reporting packs that include technology risk dashboards, incident summaries, vulnerability metrics, and risk treatment progress updates
requirements.rationale: Consistent, structured reporting enables the committee to track trends over time and identify emerging issues before they escalate into material incidents

requirements.requirement: Provide the committee with access to technology risk management tools and platforms for independent verification of reported metrics when requested
requirements.rationale: Committee members may need to validate management-reported data; access to source systems supports the independent oversight role expected by BNM

governance.summary: Formal governance arrangements must ensure the committee operates with proper authority, documented processes, and qualified membership to fulfil its RMiT oversight mandate.

requirements.requirement: Document and maintain committee terms of reference specifying membership composition, quorum requirements, meeting frequency, reporting lines to the full board, and authority to commission independent reviews
requirements.rationale: Clear terms of reference are the primary document BNM examiners review to assess whether the committee has the mandate and authority to fulfil its oversight role effectively

requirements.requirement: Maintain documented profiles of all committee members demonstrating relevant technology, cybersecurity, or risk management expertise through qualifications, certifications, or professional experience
requirements.rationale: BNM explicitly requires committee members to have sufficient knowledge in technology and cybersecurity; inability to demonstrate this competency is a common examination finding

requirements.requirement: Maintain complete records of meeting attendance, ensuring quorum is consistently achieved and that attendance rates are reported to the full board annually
requirements.rationale: Poor attendance undermines the committee's effectiveness and signals to BNM that technology risk oversight is not taken seriously at the board level

8.3.subsection: Responsibilities of the Board of Directors
8.3.title: Board-Level Technology Committee

business.summary: Board members who lack technology and cybersecurity knowledge cannot effectively challenge management or make informed decisions about technology investments, risk appetite, and incident response, exposing the institution to poorly governed technology risk.

requirements.requirement: Conduct an annual board skills assessment to identify technology and cybersecurity knowledge gaps and develop a targeted training plan to address them
requirements.rationale: Without understanding their own competency gaps, the board cannot take corrective action; BNM expects the board to proactively invest in its collective technology literacy

requirements.requirement: Engage qualified external technology and cybersecurity advisors to provide independent counsel to the board on complex technology risk matters
requirements.rationale: Not every board member will become a technology expert; external advisors bridge competency gaps and provide the independent perspective needed for effective board challenge
requirements.frequency: Annual engagement review, Ad hoc as needed

requirements.requirement: Include technology and cybersecurity competency as a criterion in the board nomination and selection process for new directors
requirements.rationale: Proactively recruiting directors with technology expertise is more effective than retroactively training directors without any technology background, and demonstrates forward-looking governance to BNM

technology.summary: Technology leaders must support board education by delivering accessible briefings, organising hands-on demonstrations, and translating complex technical concepts into business-relevant language.

requirements.requirement: Develop and deliver at least two board education sessions annually on emerging technology risks, threat landscape updates, and cybersecurity trends relevant to Malaysian financial services
requirements.rationale: Regular education sessions keep the board informed of evolving threats such as ransomware, supply chain attacks, and AI-driven fraud that require board-level awareness and strategic response

requirements.requirement: Provide the board with access to curated threat intelligence briefings and industry reports tailored to the Malaysian banking sector
requirements.rationale: Board members need context-specific information about threats targeting Malaysian financial institutions to make informed risk appetite and investment decisions

governance.summary: Formal governance mechanisms must ensure board technology competency is systematically assessed, developed, and maintained through structured training programmes and documented competency frameworks.

requirements.requirement: Establish a Board Technology Competency Framework that defines expected knowledge areas, proficiency levels, and assessment criteria for all board members
requirements.rationale: A structured framework transforms competency development from ad hoc to systematic, providing BNM with clear evidence that the institution takes board technology literacy seriously

requirements.requirement: Maintain detailed training records for all board members documenting attendance at technology and cybersecurity education sessions, external conferences, and certification programmes
requirements.rationale: Training records are primary evidence for BNM examiners assessing compliance with this guideline; without them, the institution cannot demonstrate ongoing board development

requirements.requirement: Report the results of board competency assessments and training completion rates to the Board Nomination Committee annually for inclusion in the annual governance review
requirements.rationale: Embedding competency results into the governance review process creates accountability and ensures technology knowledge gaps are addressed through the established board development pipeline

8.4.subsection: Responsibilities of the Board of Directors
8.4.title: Board Discussion on Technology and Cyber Risk

business.summary: A clearly defined technology risk appetite statement ensures the institution takes calculated technology risks that align with its strategic objectives and regulatory obligations, preventing both excessive risk-taking and overly conservative approaches that hinder digital transformation.

requirements.requirement: Develop and obtain board approval for a technology risk appetite statement that defines acceptable levels of risk across cybersecurity, system availability, data integrity, and third-party technology dependencies
requirements.rationale: Without a board-approved risk appetite, management has no authoritative guidance on how much technology risk is acceptable, leading to inconsistent risk decisions across the institution

requirements.requirement: Express risk appetite using both quantitative metrics (e.g., maximum acceptable downtime, incident thresholds, recovery time objectives) and qualitative statements (e.g., zero tolerance for data breaches affecting customer records)
requirements.rationale: BNM explicitly requires both quantitative and qualitative measures; qualitative-only statements are too vague to operationalise, while numbers alone miss important nuances

requirements.requirement: Cascade the board-approved technology risk appetite into operational risk tolerances and limits for business units, IT, and cybersecurity teams
requirements.rationale: A risk appetite statement sitting in a boardroom document achieves nothing unless it is translated into operational limits that guide day-to-day decision-making across the institution

technology.summary: Technology teams must implement monitoring systems that continuously measure actual risk levels against approved appetite thresholds and trigger alerts when limits are approached or breached.

requirements.requirement: Implement automated monitoring dashboards that track actual technology risk levels against approved appetite thresholds in real time or near-real time
requirements.rationale: Manual tracking of risk appetite adherence is too slow to detect breaches in a rapidly evolving threat environment; automated monitoring enables timely escalation and response

requirements.requirement: Configure alert mechanisms that notify risk management and senior management when technology risk indicators approach or breach defined appetite thresholds
requirements.rationale: Proactive alerting when approaching thresholds allows corrective action before a formal breach occurs, demonstrating mature risk management to BNM

requirements.requirement: Maintain historical data on risk appetite adherence to support trend analysis and inform annual risk appetite recalibration
requirements.rationale: Historical trend data enables the board to make evidence-based decisions when reviewing and adjusting risk appetite, rather than relying on subjective assessments

governance.summary: Governance processes must ensure the risk appetite statement is formally approved, regularly reviewed, effectively communicated, and used as the basis for risk decision-making throughout the institution.

requirements.requirement: Conduct a formal annual review of the technology risk appetite statement to ensure it reflects changes in the operating environment, threat landscape, regulatory requirements, and business strategy
requirements.rationale: A static risk appetite statement becomes irrelevant as the institution's technology landscape evolves; annual review ensures alignment with current realities and BNM expectations

requirements.requirement: Document and distribute the approved risk appetite statement to all relevant stakeholders including business unit heads, IT leadership, and risk management teams with formal acknowledgement
requirements.rationale: Risk appetite is only effective if the people making technology risk decisions are aware of and understand the board-approved boundaries within which they must operate

requirements.requirement: Establish a formal process for risk appetite threshold breaches that includes root cause analysis, remediation planning, and board notification within defined timeframes
requirements.rationale: Threshold breaches without a structured response process indicate that the risk appetite framework is not operationally embedded, which is a significant BNM examination finding

8.5.subsection: Responsibilities of the Board of Directors
8.5.title: Board Audit Committee Technology Oversight

business.summary: Without comprehensive and timely technology risk reporting, the board operates blind to cyber threats, system vulnerabilities, and remediation gaps that could result in service disruptions, customer harm, or BNM enforcement actions.

requirements.requirement: Ensure senior management delivers comprehensive quarterly technology risk reports to the board covering key risk indicators, incident summaries, risk mitigation progress, and forward-looking threat assessments
requirements.rationale: Quarterly reporting is the BNM-mandated minimum cadence; less frequent reporting creates unacceptable gaps in board awareness of the institution's technology risk posture

requirements.requirement: Include trend analysis in all board technology risk reports showing directional movement of key risk indicators over at least four quarters
requirements.rationale: Point-in-time snapshots do not reveal whether the institution's technology risk posture is improving or deteriorating; trend analysis enables the board to assess the effectiveness of risk management investments

requirements.requirement: Include forward-looking assessments in board reports covering emerging technology threats, planned technology changes, and their potential impact on the institution's risk profile
requirements.rationale: BNM expects boards to be proactive, not reactive; forward-looking assessments demonstrate that the institution anticipates and prepares for evolving technology risks rather than only responding to incidents

technology.summary: Technology and security teams must build robust data collection, analytics, and reporting capabilities to produce accurate, timely, and board-appropriate technology risk reports.

requirements.requirement: Implement a centralised key risk indicator dashboard that aggregates data from security, infrastructure, and application monitoring systems into a unified board reporting view
requirements.rationale: Fragmented data across multiple systems leads to inconsistent and potentially inaccurate board reporting; a centralised dashboard ensures a single source of truth for technology risk metrics

requirements.requirement: Automate the collection and correlation of technology risk data to minimise manual data gathering and reduce the risk of reporting errors or delays
requirements.rationale: Manual data collection for board reports is error-prone and time-consuming; automation ensures accuracy and allows the risk team to focus on analysis rather than data aggregation

requirements.requirement: Develop incident summary reporting capabilities that categorise technology incidents by severity, root cause, business impact, and resolution status for board consumption
requirements.rationale: The board needs structured incident data to understand patterns, assess the effectiveness of incident response, and make informed decisions about investing in preventive controls

governance.summary: Formal governance processes must ensure board reports are standardised, timely, reviewed for accuracy, and result in documented board acknowledgement and action.

requirements.requirement: Establish a standardised board reporting template for technology risk that covers all mandatory elements: KRIs, incident summaries, risk mitigation status, trend analysis, and forward-looking assessments
requirements.rationale: A standardised template ensures consistency across reporting periods and prevents omission of critical information elements required by BNM

requirements.requirement: Implement a quality assurance process for board technology risk reports including review by the CISO and CRO before submission to ensure accuracy and completeness
requirements.rationale: Inaccurate or incomplete board reports undermine trust and can lead to poor board decisions; a formal QA process ensures the board receives reliable information

requirements.requirement: Document board acknowledgement and any resulting actions or directives arising from technology risk reports in board meeting minutes
requirements.rationale: BNM examiners look for evidence that the board not only receives reports but engages with the content and directs action; undocumented acknowledgement is equivalent to no acknowledgement

8.6.title: Senior Management Accountability for Technology Risk

business.summary: Clear senior management accountability for technology risk ensures that the board-approved framework is operationally implemented, with a designated CISO providing independent cybersecurity leadership and direct board access.

requirements.requirement: Define and document a senior management accountability matrix that assigns specific technology risk responsibilities to named C-suite executives with clear ownership boundaries
requirements.rationale: Ambiguous accountability leads to gaps in technology risk management where critical activities fall between roles; BNM expects named individuals accountable for specific risk domains

requirements.requirement: Formally appoint a Chief Information Security Officer or equivalent role with a mandate covering cybersecurity strategy, incident response oversight, and security architecture governance
requirements.rationale: The CISO role is explicitly required by BNM RMiT; absence of a formally appointed CISO with a clear mandate is a direct compliance gap and a common examination finding

requirements.requirement: Ensure the CISO has a direct reporting line to the board or board technology risk committee for cybersecurity matters, independent of the CTO or Head of IT reporting structure
requirements.rationale: If the CISO reports only through the CTO, there is an inherent conflict of interest as the technology delivery function controls the security oversight function; BNM requires independent access to the board

technology.summary: Technology leadership must implement the operational structures, reporting mechanisms, and performance management systems that translate senior management accountability into effective day-to-day technology risk management.

requirements.requirement: Implement a technology risk management reporting system that provides the CISO with real-time visibility across all technology risk domains to support board and committee reporting obligations
requirements.rationale: The CISO cannot fulfil board reporting responsibilities without comprehensive visibility into the institution's technology risk posture across all domains including infrastructure, applications, and third parties

requirements.requirement: Ensure the CISO has administrative and audit access to all security tools, SIEM platforms, and vulnerability management systems to independently verify the institution's security posture
requirements.rationale: A CISO who cannot independently verify security data reported by operational teams cannot provide credible assurance to the board; tool access supports the independence requirement

governance.summary: Governance structures must formalise senior management accountability through documented roles, reporting lines, and performance frameworks that demonstrate compliance with BNM's expectations for technology risk leadership.

requirements.requirement: Document the CISO's appointment, reporting lines, mandate, and authority in a formal board-approved mandate document that is reviewed annually
requirements.rationale: A formal mandate document is the primary evidence BNM examiners seek to verify that the CISO role has been established with appropriate authority and independence as required by RMiT

requirements.requirement: Include technology risk management performance objectives in senior management KPIs and performance assessments to reinforce accountability
requirements.rationale: Accountability without performance consequences is hollow; embedding technology risk objectives in KPIs ensures senior leaders are personally invested in effective risk management

requirements.requirement: Maintain records of CISO presentations and reports to the board or board committee, demonstrating that the direct reporting line is exercised in practice, not just on paper
requirements.rationale: BNM examiners will verify that the CISO's board reporting line is actively used; a reporting line that exists in the organisation chart but is never exercised fails to meet the intent of the requirement

business.summary: An independent technology risk management function acts as the institution's second line of defence, providing objective risk identification, assessment, and reporting that is free from the conflicts inherent in technology operations teams managing their own risks.

requirements.requirement: Establish and fund a dedicated technology risk management function that is organisationally independent from IT operations, with its own budget, staffing, and reporting line
requirements.rationale: If technology risk management reports to the same leader who manages IT operations, there is an inherent conflict of interest; independence ensures objective risk assessment and reporting

requirements.requirement: Staff the technology risk management function with personnel who possess relevant risk management, cybersecurity, and technology audit competencies proportionate to the institution's technology footprint
requirements.rationale: An understaffed or underqualified function cannot effectively identify and assess technology risks across the institution's growing technology estate, creating blind spots that BNM will identify during examinations

requirements.requirement: Ensure the function has unrestricted access to all technology systems, data, documentation, and personnel necessary to perform its risk identification and assessment activities
requirements.rationale: A risk function that cannot access the systems and information it needs to assess risk is functionally ineffective; BNM expects demonstrated access authority, not just a paper mandate

technology.summary: The technology risk management function requires dedicated tools, platforms, and system access to independently identify, assess, monitor, and report technology risks across the institution.

requirements.requirement: Provide the technology risk management function with a dedicated GRC platform or risk management toolset for documenting risk assessments, tracking treatment plans, and generating risk reports
requirements.rationale: Spreadsheet-based risk management is error-prone and does not scale; a dedicated platform enables consistent methodology application, audit trails, and efficient reporting to the board

requirements.requirement: Grant the function read-only access to technology monitoring systems including SIEM, vulnerability scanners, and configuration management databases for independent risk verification
requirements.rationale: Independent access to operational monitoring data enables the risk function to verify claims made by IT operations and identify risks that operational teams may not self-report

governance.summary: Formal governance arrangements must establish the function's mandate, authority, independence, and reporting obligations through documented charters and organisational structures approved by the board.

requirements.requirement: Establish a formal charter for the technology risk management function that defines its mandate, authority, independence, scope, and reporting obligations, approved by the board or board committee
requirements.rationale: The charter is the foundational document that BNM examiners review to assess whether the function has the authority and independence required by RMiT; without it, the function's legitimacy is questionable

requirements.requirement: Ensure the function's organisational placement demonstrates clear independence from IT operations through separate reporting lines, as evidenced in the organisation chart
requirements.rationale: Independence is not just about the charter; BNM examiners will verify the actual organisation structure to confirm the function does not report to IT operations leadership

requirements.requirement: Maintain a staffing plan and competency matrix for the function that maps required competencies against current staff capabilities and identifies development needs
requirements.rationale: BNM expects the function to have adequate expertise; a competency matrix demonstrates systematic capability management and highlights any gaps that need to be addressed through hiring or training

requirements.requirement: Define and implement regular reporting from the technology risk function to the CRO, senior management, and the board committee covering risk assessments, emerging risks, and function performance metrics
requirements.rationale: Regular reporting demonstrates that the function is active and producing value; BNM will question a function that exists on paper but produces no visible output or reporting

9.1.title: TRMF Integration with Enterprise Risk Management

business.summary: A comprehensive technology risk management framework provides the foundational structure for systematically identifying, assessing, treating, and monitoring technology risks in a manner proportionate to the institution's size, complexity, and risk profile.

requirements.requirement: Develop and maintain a technology risk management framework that is proportionate to the institution's nature, size, complexity, and technology dependencies
requirements.rationale: A one-size-fits-all framework either overwhelms smaller institutions or leaves larger ones underprotected; BNM expects the framework to be calibrated to the institution's actual risk exposure

requirements.requirement: Align the technology risk management framework with the enterprise risk management framework to ensure technology risks are considered alongside credit, market, operational, and strategic risks
requirements.rationale: Technology risks do not exist in isolation; a cyber incident can trigger operational losses, reputational damage, and regulatory penalties simultaneously, requiring an integrated risk view

requirements.requirement: Obtain formal board approval for the technology risk management framework and any material changes to its structure, scope, or methodology
requirements.rationale: Board approval demonstrates that the highest governance body has reviewed and endorsed the approach to managing technology risk, which is a fundamental BNM expectation

technology.summary: The framework must be supported by technology tools and systems that enable consistent risk assessment, centralised risk data management, and automated reporting across the institution.

requirements.requirement: Implement a GRC platform or equivalent tool to operationalise the framework, enabling consistent risk assessments, centralised risk data, automated workflows, and integrated reporting
requirements.rationale: Manual or spreadsheet-based framework implementation does not scale, creates data quality issues, and makes it difficult to demonstrate consistent methodology application to BNM examiners

requirements.requirement: Integrate the technology risk framework with existing IT service management, security monitoring, and asset management systems to enable automated risk data collection
requirements.rationale: Integration reduces manual data entry, improves data accuracy, and ensures the framework captures risks from operational systems in near-real time

governance.summary: The framework must define clear governance structures, roles, methodologies, and reporting mechanisms that establish how technology risk is managed from board level through to operational teams.

requirements.requirement: Define within the framework the governance structures for technology risk including committee structures, roles and responsibilities, escalation paths, and reporting lines from operational level to the board
requirements.rationale: Clear governance structures prevent confusion about who makes risk decisions at each level and ensure risks are escalated appropriately from operational teams to senior management and the board

requirements.requirement: Document the risk appetite framework within the technology risk management framework, including how risk appetite is defined, cascaded, monitored, and enforced across the institution
requirements.rationale: The risk appetite component connects strategic risk tolerance set by the board to operational risk limits enforced by management, which is a critical linkage BNM examiners expect to see

requirements.requirement: Specify the risk assessment methodology within the framework including risk identification approaches, likelihood and impact rating scales, risk scoring methodology, and assessment frequency requirements
requirements.rationale: A standardised methodology ensures consistency across risk assessments conducted by different teams and enables meaningful aggregation and comparison of risks across the institution

requirements.requirement: Conduct an annual review of the framework to assess its effectiveness, currency, and alignment with changes in the regulatory environment, business strategy, and technology landscape
requirements.rationale: An outdated framework provides false assurance; annual reviews ensure the framework evolves with the institution's technology footprint and BNM's regulatory expectations

business.summary: Systematic identification and assessment of technology risks across all critical assets and services is essential for understanding the institution's true risk exposure and making informed decisions about risk treatment investments and priorities.

requirements.requirement: Conduct comprehensive technology risk assessments at least annually covering all critical technology assets, systems, and services, considering threats, vulnerabilities, likelihood, and potential business impact
requirements.rationale: Annual assessments are the BNM-mandated minimum; without them, the institution lacks a current understanding of its technology risk exposure, leading to uninformed resource allocation and potential regulatory findings

requirements.requirement: Trigger ad hoc risk assessments upon significant changes including major system implementations, infrastructure migrations, organisational restructuring, mergers and acquisitions, and material security incidents
requirements.rationale: Annual assessments alone are insufficient when the technology environment changes significantly between cycles; change-triggered assessments ensure the risk picture remains current and accurate

requirements.requirement: Quantify the potential business impact of identified technology risks in terms of financial loss, operational disruption, customer impact, regulatory exposure, and reputational damage
requirements.rationale: Risk assessments that do not translate technical vulnerabilities into business impact fail to inform board-level decision-making and make it impossible to prioritise treatment investments effectively

technology.summary: Technical teams must deploy and maintain tools for continuous threat intelligence gathering, vulnerability scanning, and asset discovery to feed accurate data into the risk assessment process.

requirements.requirement: Maintain a comprehensive and current inventory of all technology assets including hardware, software, cloud services, APIs, and data repositories that serves as the scope baseline for risk assessments
requirements.rationale: Risk assessments can only cover known assets; an incomplete or outdated asset inventory means the institution has blind spots where unassessed risks may reside

requirements.requirement: Deploy automated vulnerability scanning tools that regularly assess technical vulnerabilities across the institution's infrastructure, applications, and cloud environments
requirements.rationale: Automated scanning provides objective, repeatable data on technical vulnerabilities that feeds directly into risk assessments and ensures no critical vulnerabilities are missed

requirements.requirement: Subscribe to and integrate threat intelligence feeds relevant to the Malaysian financial services sector to inform threat identification during risk assessments
requirements.rationale: Risk assessments must consider current and emerging threats; Malaysian-specific threat intelligence ensures assessments reflect the actual threat actors and attack vectors targeting the local financial sector

governance.summary: A documented risk assessment methodology with defined rating scales, assessment procedures, and quality assurance processes ensures consistency, repeatability, and auditability of technology risk assessments.

requirements.requirement: Document a formal technology risk assessment methodology that defines risk identification techniques, likelihood and impact rating scales, risk scoring calculations, and assessment procedures
requirements.rationale: A documented methodology ensures consistency across assessments conducted by different teams and enables BNM to verify that the institution applies a systematic, repeatable approach to risk assessment

requirements.requirement: Maintain a risk assessment schedule that plans assessments across all critical technology domains and tracks completion status, ensuring full coverage within each annual cycle
requirements.rationale: Without a schedule, risk assessments tend to focus on familiar areas while neglecting others; a planned schedule ensures comprehensive coverage and provides BNM with evidence of systematic execution

requirements.requirement: Implement a quality assurance process for completed risk assessments including peer review, methodology compliance checks, and sign-off by the Head of Technology Risk
requirements.rationale: Risk assessments vary in quality depending on the assessor's experience; quality assurance ensures assessments meet defined standards and produce reliable, comparable results across the institution

business.summary: Effective risk treatment ensures that every identified technology risk has a deliberate response -- whether avoided, mitigated, transferred, or formally accepted -- with clear accountability, timelines, and approval at the appropriate authority level.

requirements.requirement: Develop risk treatment plans for all identified technology risks specifying the chosen treatment option (avoid, mitigate, transfer, or accept), implementation timelines, resource requirements, and the named individual accountable for execution
requirements.rationale: Identified risks without treatment plans represent unmanaged exposure; BNM expects every risk to have a deliberate, documented response with clear accountability

requirements.requirement: Establish a formal risk acceptance process requiring sign-off at the appropriate authority level based on the risk's severity, with board-level approval required for high and critical residual risks
requirements.rationale: Risk acceptance without proper authority is a governance failure; senior management or the board must consciously decide to accept risks above defined thresholds, with full understanding of the potential consequences

requirements.requirement: Allocate sufficient budget and resources for risk treatment implementation, with tracking mechanisms to ensure treatment plans are funded and progressing on schedule
requirements.rationale: Treatment plans without funding are aspirational; the institution must demonstrate that it commits real resources to reducing identified technology risks within agreed timelines

technology.summary: Technology teams must implement the technical controls, system changes, and security measures specified in risk treatment plans, with verifiable evidence of implementation effectiveness.

requirements.requirement: Implement technical controls and security measures specified in risk treatment plans with documented evidence of deployment, configuration, and effectiveness testing
requirements.rationale: A treatment plan that specifies a technical control is only effective once the control is properly implemented, configured, and tested; deployment without verification provides false assurance

requirements.requirement: Conduct post-implementation validation of risk mitigation controls to verify they reduce the identified risk to within acceptable levels as defined in the treatment plan
requirements.rationale: Controls may be implemented but may not be effective in actually reducing the risk; post-implementation validation confirms the treatment achieved its intended risk reduction

requirements.requirement: Maintain ongoing monitoring of implemented controls to ensure they continue to operate effectively and the residual risk remains within the accepted level
requirements.rationale: Controls can degrade over time due to configuration drift, technology changes, or evolving threats; ongoing monitoring ensures continued effectiveness

governance.summary: Governance processes must define risk treatment policies, approval authorities, progress tracking mechanisms, and reporting requirements to ensure treatment plans are executed effectively and transparently.

requirements.requirement: Document a risk treatment policy that defines the available treatment options, approval authority levels for each risk severity tier, and requirements for treatment plan documentation
requirements.rationale: A formal policy ensures consistent treatment decision-making across the institution and provides BNM examiners with a clear framework for assessing whether appropriate approvals were obtained

requirements.requirement: Implement a treatment plan tracking system that monitors progress against timelines, escalates overdue plans to senior management, and reports status to the board quarterly
requirements.rationale: Without tracking, treatment plans stagnate; systematic monitoring with escalation ensures accountability and provides the board with visibility into whether the institution is actually reducing its technology risk exposure

requirements.requirement: Maintain a formal record of all risk acceptance decisions including the risk description, residual risk level, business justification, approving authority, approval date, and next review date
requirements.rationale: Risk acceptance records are a primary audit artefact; BNM examiners will verify that accepted risks were approved at the correct authority level with documented justification and periodic revalidation

9.4.title: Designation of Chief Information Security Officer

business.summary: Continuous monitoring and structured reporting of technology risks ensures the institution detects emerging threats and risk appetite breaches early, enabling timely management response and informed board oversight.

requirements.requirement: Define and implement key risk indicators for all critical technology risk domains, with specific thresholds that trigger escalation when risk appetite limits are approached or breached
requirements.rationale: KRIs translate abstract risk concepts into measurable metrics that management and the board can monitor; without defined thresholds, it is impossible to determine whether the institution is operating within its stated risk appetite

requirements.requirement: Establish periodic technology risk reporting to senior management and the board that includes KRI performance, emerging risk alerts, and risk treatment progress
requirements.rationale: Regular reporting creates a feedback loop between operational risk monitoring and strategic decision-making, ensuring management and the board remain informed and can intervene when needed
requirements.frequency: Monthly to senior management, Quarterly to board

requirements.requirement: Integrate technology risk monitoring data into the enterprise-wide risk reporting framework to provide a holistic view of the institution's risk profile
requirements.rationale: Technology risks that are reported in isolation cannot be properly weighted against other institutional risks; integration ensures the board sees the full picture when making strategic risk decisions

technology.summary: Technology infrastructure must support continuous, automated monitoring of risk indicators through integrated dashboards, SIEM platforms, and alerting systems that provide real-time visibility into the institution's risk posture.

requirements.requirement: Deploy automated monitoring tools that continuously collect and correlate data for defined KRIs across infrastructure, applications, security, and third-party technology services
requirements.rationale: Manual KRI data collection is too slow and error-prone for effective risk monitoring; automated tools ensure timely, accurate data that can trigger alerts before thresholds are breached

requirements.requirement: Implement a centralised risk monitoring dashboard that consolidates KRI data from multiple sources and provides configurable threshold alerts for risk management and senior management
requirements.rationale: A centralised dashboard provides a single pane of glass for risk monitoring, eliminating the need to check multiple systems and reducing the risk of missing critical threshold breaches

requirements.requirement: Configure automated escalation alerts that notify designated personnel when KRI thresholds are approached (amber) or breached (red), with different notification channels based on severity
requirements.rationale: Automated escalation removes human delay from the notification process and ensures threshold breaches are communicated to the right people immediately, enabling rapid response

governance.summary: Governance processes must define KRI ownership, escalation procedures, reporting cadences, and accountability mechanisms to ensure monitoring activities translate into timely and effective risk management actions.

requirements.requirement: Document formal escalation procedures that define the actions required when KRI thresholds are approached or breached, including notification recipients, response timeframes, and required remediation steps
requirements.rationale: Without documented escalation procedures, threshold breaches may go unaddressed or be handled inconsistently; formal procedures ensure a predictable, auditable response to risk appetite violations

requirements.requirement: Assign named owners to each key risk indicator who are accountable for data quality, threshold calibration, and escalation when their KRI approaches or breaches the defined limit
requirements.rationale: Ownerless KRIs receive no attention; assigning named owners creates personal accountability and ensures someone is responsible for monitoring and acting on each indicator

requirements.requirement: Maintain records of all KRI threshold breach events including the breach details, escalation actions taken, root cause analysis, and remediation outcomes
requirements.rationale: Breach records provide BNM examiners with evidence that the monitoring and escalation framework operates in practice, not just in theory; they also enable trend analysis to identify systemic issues

business.summary: A comprehensive, up-to-date technology risk register serves as the central repository of all identified technology risks and their treatment status, providing the institution with a single source of truth for risk-informed decision-making at all levels.

requirements.requirement: Maintain a comprehensive technology risk register that documents all identified technology risks including risk description, assessed likelihood and impact, risk owner, treatment plan, and current status
requirements.rationale: The risk register is the operational backbone of the technology risk management framework; without a complete register, the institution cannot demonstrate systematic risk management to BNM

requirements.requirement: Conduct quarterly reviews of the risk register to validate that risk assessments remain current, treatment plans are progressing, and new risks are captured in a timely manner
requirements.rationale: Quarterly review is the BNM-mandated minimum frequency; a risk register that is not regularly reviewed becomes stale and provides false assurance about the institution's actual risk exposure

requirements.requirement: Assign a named risk owner to every entry in the risk register who is accountable for monitoring the risk, implementing the treatment plan, and reporting on status changes
requirements.rationale: Risks without owners are nobody's responsibility; named ownership creates personal accountability and ensures each risk receives ongoing attention and management

technology.summary: The risk register must be maintained on a platform that supports version control, audit trails, automated workflows, and integration with other risk management tools to ensure data integrity and operational efficiency.

requirements.requirement: Maintain the risk register on a GRC platform or equivalent tool that provides version control, audit trails, automated workflow capabilities, and role-based access controls
requirements.rationale: Spreadsheet-based risk registers lack the version control and audit trail capabilities that BNM examiners expect; a proper platform demonstrates mature risk management operations

requirements.requirement: Integrate the risk register platform with vulnerability scanning, incident management, and change management systems to enable automated risk data updates and cross-referencing
requirements.rationale: Integration reduces manual effort, improves data accuracy, and ensures the register reflects findings from operational systems such as new vulnerabilities or incidents

requirements.requirement: Enable automated reporting and dashboard generation from the risk register to support quarterly board reporting and operational risk management decisions
requirements.rationale: Automated reporting from the register ensures consistency between the risk register content and board reports, eliminating transcription errors and reducing report preparation time

governance.summary: Governance processes must ensure the risk register is maintained with rigour through defined update procedures, quarterly review protocols, risk owner accountability mechanisms, and comprehensive change history.

requirements.requirement: Establish formal procedures for adding, updating, escalating, and closing risks in the register, including required fields, approval workflows, and documentation standards
requirements.rationale: Without standardised procedures, risk register entries vary in quality and completeness, undermining the register's value as a reliable risk management tool

requirements.requirement: Document quarterly risk register review outcomes including risks reviewed, status changes, new risks added, risks closed, and any escalations to senior management or the board
requirements.rationale: Documented review outcomes demonstrate to BNM examiners that quarterly reviews are substantive assessments, not perfunctory exercises, and provide an audit trail of risk register evolution

requirements.requirement: Require risk owners to formally acknowledge their assigned risks and provide quarterly status updates on treatment plan progress and any changes to risk levels
requirements.rationale: Formal acknowledgement creates documented accountability; quarterly updates from risk owners ensure the register reflects current conditions rather than outdated assessments

requirements.requirement: Maintain a complete change history for the risk register documenting all additions, modifications, and closures with timestamps, authors, and justifications
requirements.rationale: Change history provides BNM examiners with an audit trail that demonstrates the register is actively maintained and enables investigation of how risk assessments evolved over time

business.summary: Without a formal IT project governance framework, major technology initiatives risk budget overruns, scope creep, and misalignment with business objectives, potentially disrupting customer services and regulatory commitments.

requirements.requirement: Define and communicate criteria for classifying technology projects as major, including thresholds for budget, customer impact, regulatory scope, and strategic significance
requirements.rationale: Clear classification ensures that high-impact projects receive proportionate oversight and resource allocation, preventing costly failures from inadequate governance

requirements.requirement: Allocate dedicated resources (budget, personnel, and tools) for IT project governance activities including independent risk assessments and stage-gate reviews
requirements.rationale: Governance activities require ring-fenced resources to avoid being deprioritised when delivery pressures mount

requirements.requirement: Ensure all major technology projects have a documented business case with measurable success criteria approved by senior management before commencement
requirements.rationale: Business case discipline prevents investment in projects that lack clear strategic value or customer benefit

technology.summary: Technology teams must implement project management tooling and workflows that enforce stage-gate approvals, maintain audit trails, and integrate with risk assessment processes.

requirements.requirement: Implement a project management platform (e.g., Jira, Azure DevOps, MS Project) with configured stage-gate workflows that enforce mandatory approvals before phase transitions
requirements.rationale: Automated workflows prevent projects from advancing without completing required governance checkpoints, creating a verifiable audit trail

requirements.requirement: Maintain a centralised project portfolio dashboard that provides real-time visibility into project status, risk ratings, milestone completion, and resource utilisation
requirements.rationale: Centralised visibility enables senior management to identify troubled projects early and intervene before they impact operations

requirements.requirement: Configure automated alerts and escalation triggers when projects breach defined thresholds for schedule variance, budget overrun, or unresolved risk items
requirements.rationale: Proactive alerting ensures governance issues are surfaced to decision-makers in a timely manner rather than discovered during periodic reviews

governance.summary: A documented governance framework must define roles, responsibilities, approval gates, and oversight mechanisms for all technology projects, with enhanced controls for major initiatives.

requirements.requirement: Establish and maintain a board-approved IT Project Governance Framework that defines project classification criteria, roles and responsibilities, stage-gate approval requirements, and escalation procedures
requirements.rationale: A formally approved framework provides the authoritative basis for all project governance activities and demonstrates regulatory compliance to BNM examiners

requirements.requirement: Require independent risk assessments for all projects classified as major, conducted by personnel not directly involved in project delivery, with results presented to the board or senior management for approval
requirements.rationale: Independence in risk assessment prevents conflicts of interest where project teams may downplay risks to secure approval

requirements.requirement: Mandate quarterly reporting to the board or IT steering committee on the technology project portfolio, including status of major projects, governance compliance metrics, and risk exposure summary
requirements.rationale: Regular board-level reporting ensures sustained oversight and accountability, enabling timely strategic decisions on project continuation or termination

requirements.requirement: Review and update the IT Project Governance Framework at least annually or following significant organisational changes, regulatory updates, or lessons learned from project failures
requirements.rationale: Periodic review ensures the framework remains current and incorporates improvements from experience and evolving regulatory expectations

business.summary: Failing to conduct thorough risk assessments for technology projects exposes the institution to unidentified threats to security, system availability, and data integrity, potentially causing service disruptions and customer harm.

requirements.requirement: Ensure every technology project includes a risk assessment that explicitly evaluates potential impacts on customer-facing services, business operations, and regulatory obligations before project approval
requirements.rationale: Business impact awareness at the project planning stage prevents costly remediation and service disruptions after deployment

requirements.requirement: Require project risk assessments to include interdependency analysis identifying all upstream and downstream systems, shared infrastructure, and third-party dependencies affected by the project
requirements.rationale: Unidentified interdependencies are a leading cause of project-related incidents in Malaysian financial institutions where legacy systems are tightly coupled

requirements.requirement: Establish risk appetite thresholds for technology projects that trigger escalation to senior management or the board when residual risk exceeds acceptable levels
requirements.rationale: Clear escalation thresholds ensure that projects carrying excessive risk receive appropriate management attention and decision-making

technology.summary: Technical teams must implement structured risk assessment processes that systematically identify and evaluate risks to security, availability, and data integrity across all project phases.

requirements.requirement: Implement a standardised project risk assessment template that covers security risks, availability impacts, data integrity threats, system interdependencies, and compliance requirements
requirements.rationale: Standardised templates ensure consistent and comprehensive risk identification across all projects regardless of project manager experience

requirements.requirement: Conduct technical interdependency mapping for each project using architecture diagrams, system integration maps, and CMDB data to identify all affected systems and interfaces
requirements.rationale: Systematic technical mapping prevents overlooking critical dependencies that could cause cascading failures during or after implementation

requirements.requirement: Integrate project risk assessment findings into project plans with specific risk mitigation tasks, assigned owners, target completion dates, and verification criteria
requirements.rationale: Risk mitigations must be actionable project tasks to ensure they are implemented rather than remaining as documented intentions

governance.summary: A formal risk assessment methodology must be established for all technology projects, with documented outputs reviewed and approved by appropriate authorities before project commencement.

requirements.requirement: Establish and maintain a formal project risk assessment methodology that defines the assessment process, risk categories, scoring criteria, and documentation requirements applicable to all technology projects
requirements.rationale: A documented methodology ensures risk assessments are conducted consistently and meet the standard expected by BNM examiners

requirements.requirement: Require risk assessment outputs to be formally reviewed and approved by the project steering committee or designated risk authority before project commencement, with risk acceptance decisions documented for residual risks
requirements.rationale: Formal approval of risk assessments establishes accountability and ensures informed decision-making on project risk exposure

requirements.requirement: Mandate periodic reassessment of project risks at each stage-gate and whenever material changes to project scope, timeline, or technology occur
requirements.rationale: Project risks evolve throughout the lifecycle; static assessments become stale and fail to capture emerging risks from scope changes or technical decisions

10.3.title: Board and Senior Management Project Reporting

business.summary: Post-implementation reviews ensure the institution captures lessons from technology projects and verifies that intended business benefits are being realised, preventing repeated costly mistakes across future initiatives.

requirements.requirement: Conduct a formal post-implementation review within three months of go-live for all significant technology projects, assessing whether stated business objectives and success criteria have been achieved
requirements.rationale: Timely review while knowledge is fresh ensures accurate assessment of outcomes and prevents benefit realisation gaps from going undetected

requirements.requirement: Include business stakeholder feedback in post-implementation reviews, covering operational impact, user adoption, and customer experience outcomes
requirements.rationale: Technical delivery success does not automatically equate to business value; business stakeholder input validates whether the project achieved its intended purpose

requirements.requirement: Track and report on post-implementation review completion rates across the project portfolio to ensure reviews are consistently conducted
requirements.rationale: Without completion tracking, post-implementation reviews are frequently deprioritised as teams move on to new projects

technology.summary: Technical teams must assess whether implemented systems meet performance, security, and reliability targets established during project planning, and identify any operational issues requiring remediation.

requirements.requirement: Evaluate system performance metrics, security control effectiveness, and operational stability during the post-implementation review, comparing actual outcomes against design targets
requirements.rationale: Technical validation confirms that the system operates as intended in production conditions and identifies any gaps requiring corrective action

requirements.requirement: Assess the effectiveness of risk mitigation measures implemented during the project by reviewing incident data, vulnerability assessments, and control test results from the post-go-live period
requirements.rationale: Risk mitigations designed during planning may not be fully effective in practice; early validation prevents prolonged exposure to inadequately controlled risks

requirements.requirement: Document all technical lessons learned including infrastructure challenges, integration issues, and deployment problems encountered, and feed these into SDLC process improvements
requirements.rationale: Systematic capture and application of technical lessons prevents recurring issues and continuously improves delivery capability

governance.summary: Post-implementation reviews must be formally governed with standardised templates, required participants, and a mechanism to ensure lessons learned are incorporated into future projects.

requirements.requirement: Establish a standardised post-implementation review procedure that defines review timelines (within three months of go-live), required participants, assessment criteria, and documentation requirements
requirements.rationale: A standardised procedure ensures consistent quality of reviews across the portfolio and sets clear expectations for all stakeholders

requirements.requirement: Maintain a centralised lessons learned register that captures findings from all post-implementation reviews, with assigned actions for incorporating improvements into governance frameworks and SDLC processes
requirements.rationale: A centralised register transforms individual project learnings into organisational knowledge that improves future project outcomes

requirements.requirement: Report post-implementation review outcomes and key lessons learned to the IT steering committee or senior management on a quarterly basis
requirements.rationale: Management visibility ensures that systemic issues identified across multiple reviews are addressed at the appropriate organisational level

business.summary: A formal SDLC framework with integrated security checkpoints protects the institution from deploying vulnerable or defective systems that could compromise customer data, disrupt services, or trigger regulatory action.

requirements.requirement: Ensure the SDLC framework mandates business owner involvement at requirements, UAT, and deployment phases to validate that security and functional requirements meet business needs
requirements.rationale: Business engagement at critical phases prevents misalignment between technical delivery and business expectations, reducing costly rework and customer impact

requirements.requirement: Allocate budget for security activities at each SDLC phase including threat modelling, security testing, and code review tooling as standard project costs
requirements.rationale: When security activities are not budgeted, they are the first to be cut under delivery pressure, resulting in vulnerable deployments

requirements.requirement: Define and communicate clear escalation paths when quality gates cannot be satisfied, including the authority levels required to grant exceptions
requirements.rationale: Clear escalation paths prevent projects from silently bypassing quality gates while ensuring legitimate exceptions receive appropriate risk-based decisions

technology.summary: The SDLC framework must define security checkpoints and quality gates at each development phase, with specific technical activities, entry and exit criteria, and mandatory tooling requirements.

requirements.requirement: Define mandatory security activities for each SDLC phase: threat modelling at design, secure coding standards at development, SAST/DAST at testing, security configuration review at deployment, and vulnerability monitoring at maintenance
requirements.rationale: Phase-specific security activities ensure threats are addressed at the earliest and most cost-effective point in the development lifecycle

requirements.requirement: Establish measurable quality gate criteria for each phase transition including minimum code coverage, maximum critical/high vulnerability count, performance benchmarks, and security review completion
requirements.rationale: Measurable criteria remove subjectivity from gate decisions and provide clear evidence of compliance for audit purposes

requirements.requirement: Integrate security checkpoints into CI/CD pipelines as automated gates that block progression when security criteria are not met
requirements.rationale: Automation enforces consistent application of security standards without relying on manual discipline, which degrades under time pressure

governance.summary: The SDLC framework must be formally documented, approved by senior management, and subject to periodic review to ensure it remains current and is consistently applied across all development activities.

requirements.requirement: Document and obtain senior management approval for a formal SDLC framework covering all phases from requirements through maintenance, with security integrated at every phase and defined quality gates between phases
requirements.rationale: A formally approved framework establishes the authoritative standard for all development activities and demonstrates regulatory compliance to BNM

requirements.requirement: Conduct periodic compliance assessments to verify that active development projects are adhering to the SDLC framework, with findings reported to management
requirements.rationale: Framework adherence must be actively monitored because development teams under delivery pressure may take shortcuts that undermine security controls

requirements.requirement: Review and update the SDLC framework at least annually to incorporate lessons learned, new security threats, evolving technology standards, and changes to regulatory requirements
requirements.rationale: An outdated framework fails to address current threats and technologies, creating compliance gaps and security vulnerabilities

business.summary: Explicit documentation and traceability of security and compliance requirements ensures that regulatory obligations and risk controls are not lost during development, protecting the institution from deploying non-compliant systems.

requirements.requirement: Mandate that business owners collaborate with information security and compliance teams to define security and compliance requirements during the requirements gathering phase of every development project
requirements.rationale: Early engagement ensures that regulatory obligations and risk controls are captured as formal requirements rather than being retrofitted after development, which is significantly more costly

requirements.requirement: Ensure all security and compliance requirements are linked to their regulatory or policy source (e.g., specific RMiT clause, PDPA section, internal policy reference) for audit traceability
requirements.rationale: Source traceability enables the institution to demonstrate to BNM that each regulatory requirement has been translated into specific system controls

requirements.requirement: Require formal sign-off from information security and compliance functions confirming that all applicable security and compliance requirements have been captured before proceeding to design
requirements.rationale: Independent validation prevents requirements gaps that could result in systems being deployed without adequate security controls or regulatory compliance

technology.summary: Development teams must implement requirements traceability mechanisms that track each security and compliance requirement through design, implementation, testing, and validation to ensure none are dropped during development.

requirements.requirement: Implement and maintain a requirements traceability matrix (RTM) for each project that maps every security and compliance requirement to its design element, code implementation, test case, and validation result
requirements.rationale: End-to-end traceability provides objective evidence that every security requirement was implemented and verified, which is essential for BNM audit readiness

requirements.requirement: Configure project management and testing tools to enforce traceability links between requirements, user stories, test cases, and defects, preventing items without traceability from being marked complete
requirements.rationale: Tool-enforced traceability prevents requirements from being silently dropped during implementation or testing phases

requirements.requirement: Validate all security and compliance requirements through specific test cases prior to deployment, with validation results recorded in the traceability matrix
requirements.rationale: Requirements that are documented but not validated may not function as intended in production, creating a false sense of compliance

governance.summary: A formal requirements management procedure must govern how security and compliance requirements are captured, traced, validated, and approved throughout the development lifecycle.

requirements.requirement: Establish a requirements management procedure that mandates the explicit capture of security and compliance requirements for every development project, with defined roles for requirements definition, review, and approval
requirements.rationale: A formal procedure ensures consistent requirements practices across all projects and prevents security requirements from being treated as optional

requirements.requirement: Require traceability matrix completion and sign-off as a mandatory quality gate before production deployment, with incomplete traceability blocking release approval
requirements.rationale: Making traceability a deployment gate ensures that requirements validation cannot be deferred or skipped under delivery pressure

requirements.requirement: Conduct periodic audits of requirements management practices across active projects to verify compliance with the established procedure and identify improvement areas
requirements.rationale: Ongoing compliance verification ensures that requirements management practices do not degrade over time, particularly in teams under sustained delivery pressure

business.summary: Adopting and enforcing secure coding standards reduces the institution's exposure to application-level vulnerabilities that could be exploited to compromise customer data, disrupt banking services, or cause financial losses.

requirements.requirement: Allocate recurring budget for secure coding training programmes, automated scanning tool licences, and periodic standards refresh to ensure secure development capabilities remain current
requirements.rationale: Secure development requires sustained investment in people and tools; one-off training without ongoing reinforcement leads to skill degradation and non-compliance

requirements.requirement: Require all developers, including contractors and outsourced development partners, to demonstrate competency in the institution's secure coding standards before being granted access to development environments
requirements.rationale: External developers who are not trained on institutional standards introduce inconsistent code quality and potential security vulnerabilities

requirements.requirement: Include secure coding compliance metrics in development team performance reporting to reinforce accountability for code quality
requirements.rationale: Embedding security into performance metrics drives cultural change and ensures secure coding is treated as a core competency, not an afterthought

technology.summary: Development teams must adopt secure coding standards based on OWASP and industry best practices, enforce compliance through code reviews and automated scanning, and ensure all developers are adequately trained.

requirements.requirement: Adopt and maintain secure coding standards based on OWASP guidelines (OWASP Top 10, OWASP ASVS) tailored to each programming language and framework in use, covering input validation, output encoding, authentication, session management, cryptography, and error handling
requirements.rationale: Language-specific standards provide practical, actionable guidance that developers can apply directly, unlike generic security principles

requirements.requirement: Deploy and configure automated code scanning tools (SAST, SCA) integrated into the CI/CD pipeline to detect violations of secure coding standards on every code commit or pull request
requirements.rationale: Automated scanning provides continuous, consistent enforcement of coding standards at scale, catching violations before they reach code review

requirements.requirement: Conduct mandatory peer code reviews for all code changes with reviewers specifically checking against the secure coding standards checklist before approval
requirements.rationale: Manual code review catches logical security flaws and business logic vulnerabilities that automated tools cannot detect, providing defence in depth

governance.summary: Secure coding standards must be formally documented, training must be tracked and refreshed, and compliance must be monitored through reporting and periodic assessments.

requirements.requirement: Publish and maintain formally approved secure coding standards documents for each programming language in use, with annual review and update cycle aligned to OWASP guideline releases
requirements.rationale: Formally published standards provide the authoritative reference for code reviews and automated scanning rule configurations

requirements.requirement: Mandate annual secure coding training for all developers with completion tracking, and require refresher training when standards are materially updated or new vulnerability classes emerge
requirements.rationale: Regular training ensures developers maintain awareness of evolving threats and updated standards, which is a specific BNM requirement for this clause

requirements.requirement: Produce quarterly reports on secure coding compliance metrics including training completion rates, automated scan finding trends, and code review coverage to senior management
requirements.rationale: Management reporting creates accountability and enables trend analysis to identify systemic issues requiring intervention

business.summary: Mandatory code review and static analysis before production deployment prevents security vulnerabilities from reaching customer-facing systems, reducing the risk of data breaches, service outages, and reputational damage.

requirements.requirement: Establish a zero-tolerance policy for deploying code with unresolved critical or high-severity SAST findings to production, with any exceptions requiring formal risk acceptance by the CISO or CTO
requirements.rationale: Deploying known critical vulnerabilities to production creates unacceptable risk to customer data and services; formal exception processes ensure informed decision-making

requirements.requirement: Ensure code review and SAST processes apply equally to all code deployed to production including third-party customisations, integrations, and internally developed scripts
requirements.rationale: Exempting any code category from review creates blind spots that attackers can exploit; third-party code and scripts are common attack vectors

requirements.requirement: Track and report remediation timelines for SAST findings to ensure critical issues are resolved within defined SLAs and do not accumulate as technical debt
requirements.rationale: Without remediation tracking, identified vulnerabilities persist indefinitely, negating the value of the scanning investment

technology.summary: Development teams must implement mandatory peer code reviews and SAST scanning as automated pipeline gates, with defined processes for finding remediation and risk acceptance for exceptions.

requirements.requirement: Integrate SAST tools into the CI/CD pipeline as a mandatory gate that blocks deployment when critical or high-severity findings are detected, with gate bypass requiring documented approval
requirements.rationale: Pipeline-enforced gates prevent human error or time pressure from allowing vulnerable code into production

requirements.requirement: Require a minimum of one independent peer code review for all code changes before merge to the main branch, with reviewers from the approved reviewer list who have completed secure coding training
requirements.rationale: Independent peer review provides human-level security assessment that complements automated scanning, catching business logic flaws and design-level vulnerabilities

requirements.requirement: Configure SAST tools with rule sets aligned to the institution's secure coding standards and OWASP guidelines, with regular tuning to minimise false positives while maintaining detection accuracy
requirements.rationale: Poorly configured SAST tools produce excessive false positives that cause developer fatigue and lead to findings being ignored, undermining the control
