governance.summary: Formal API security standards and governance processes must ensure APIs are documented, inventoried, and subject to consistent security controls throughout their lifecycle.

requirements.requirement: Develop and maintain API Security Standards aligned with OWASP API Security Top 10, covering authentication, authorisation, rate limiting, input validation, and encryption requirements
requirements.rationale: A formal API security standard ensures consistent protection across all APIs regardless of which team develops them

requirements.requirement: Enforce mandatory API documentation (OpenAPI/Swagger specifications) and security review as prerequisites for API deployment to production
requirements.rationale: Documented APIs enable effective security assessment and ensure deprecated endpoints are properly identified

requirements.requirement: Implement an API decommissioning procedure requiring documented impact analysis, consumer notification, and confirmed migration before deprecated APIs are retired
requirements.rationale: RMiT requires timely decommissioning of deprecated APIs; a formal procedure prevents both premature and delayed retirement

12.4.subsection: Digital Fraud Management and Customer Awareness
12.4.title: Fraud Detection Capabilities

business.summary: Weak customer authentication is the most direct path to account takeover and financial fraud, making risk-proportionate authentication essential for customer protection and regulatory compliance.

requirements.requirement: Define transaction risk tiers (low, medium, high, critical) with corresponding authentication requirements, ensuring multi-factor authentication for all high-risk and critical transactions
requirements.rationale: RMiT mandates that authentication strength must be commensurate with transaction risk; a tiered approach ensures proportionate protection

requirements.requirement: Monitor authentication failure rates, account lockout events, and successful fraud cases to continuously assess authentication effectiveness and identify emerging attack patterns
requirements.rationale: Ongoing effectiveness monitoring is required by RMiT and enables early detection of credential-based attacks

requirements.requirement: Ensure customer authentication mechanisms balance security with usability, conducting periodic customer experience reviews to minimise authentication-related service abandonment
requirements.rationale: Overly complex authentication can drive customers to less secure channels or competitors, undermining both business goals and security outcomes

technology.summary: Authentication systems must support multi-factor authentication for high-risk transactions, risk-based adaptive authentication, and regular effectiveness assessments against current threat vectors.

requirements.requirement: Implement multi-factor authentication combining at least two of: knowledge (password/PIN), possession (hardware token/registered device), and inherence (biometric) for high-risk transactions
requirements.rationale: MFA for high-risk transactions is an explicit mandatory requirement under RMiT clause 12.4

requirements.requirement: Deploy risk-based adaptive authentication that evaluates device fingerprint, geolocation, transaction behaviour, and time-of-day patterns to dynamically adjust authentication requirements
requirements.rationale: Adaptive authentication enables stronger security for anomalous activities while maintaining a smooth experience for normal transactions

requirements.requirement: Implement credential protection measures including secure password hashing (bcrypt/Argon2), account lockout after failed attempts, and brute-force detection with automated blocking
requirements.rationale: Credential protection mechanisms prevent the most common authentication attack vectors targeting digital banking customers

governance.summary: A formal customer authentication policy must define risk-based authentication requirements, mandate periodic effectiveness reviews, and align with BNM's expectations for digital service security.

requirements.requirement: Establish and maintain a Customer Authentication Policy that defines risk-based authentication tiers, MFA requirements for high-risk transactions, and acceptable authentication methods
requirements.rationale: A formal policy provides the governance framework required by RMiT for customer authentication across all digital services

requirements.requirement: Conduct formal authentication effectiveness assessments at least annually, evaluating authentication mechanisms against current threats including credential stuffing, SIM swapping, and phishing
requirements.rationale: RMiT requires regular assessment of authentication effectiveness; the threat landscape evolves and authentication must keep pace

requirements.requirement: Maintain a register of all authentication mechanisms deployed across digital channels with their risk classification, last assessment date, and any identified weaknesses
requirements.rationale: A centralised authentication register enables oversight and ensures no channel is overlooked in security reviews

12.5.subsection: Digital Fraud Management and Customer Awareness
12.5.title: Digital Service Delivery Risk Mitigation

business.summary: Out-of-band transaction verification for high-value and high-risk transactions is a critical control to prevent unauthorised fund transfers and protect customers from fraud.

requirements.requirement: Define monetary and risk thresholds that trigger out-of-band transaction verification, covering fund transfers, payee additions, limit changes, and other high-risk operations
requirements.rationale: Clear thresholds ensure consistent application of verification requirements and enable customers to understand when additional verification will be required

requirements.requirement: Ensure transaction verification messages display full transaction details (amount, payee, account number) to the customer through the independent verification channel before final authorisation
requirements.rationale: RMiT explicitly requires customers to see transaction details for verification; partial information display defeats the purpose of out-of-band verification

requirements.requirement: Monitor transaction verification completion rates and investigate any anomalies such as unusually high bypass rates or verification timeout patterns
requirements.rationale: Anomalous verification patterns can indicate social engineering attacks or system weaknesses that need to be addressed

technology.summary: Transaction verification must be performed through an independent channel from the transaction initiation channel, with full transaction details presented for customer confirmation.

requirements.requirement: Implement out-of-band transaction verification using a channel independent from the transaction initiation channel (e.g., SMS OTP, push notification, or hardware token for internet banking transactions)
requirements.rationale: Channel independence is explicitly mandated by RMiT to prevent a single compromised channel from enabling fraudulent transaction authorisation

requirements.requirement: Include cryptographically bound transaction details (amount, payee, timestamp) in verification messages to prevent transaction modification attacks between verification and execution
requirements.rationale: Transaction binding ensures that the verified transaction is the same one that is executed, preventing man-in-the-middle modification

requirements.requirement: Enforce time-limited verification codes (maximum 5 minutes validity) with single-use enforcement and automatic transaction cancellation upon expiry
requirements.rationale: Time-limited and single-use codes reduce the window for interception and replay attacks on verification messages

governance.summary: Transaction verification policies must define clear thresholds, channel independence requirements, and customer notification standards for high-value and high-risk digital transactions.

requirements.requirement: Establish a Transaction Verification Policy defining which transactions require out-of-band verification, acceptable verification channels, and the transaction details that must be displayed to customers
requirements.rationale: A formal policy ensures consistent application of verification requirements across all digital channels and transaction types

requirements.requirement: Document and maintain transaction verification flow diagrams for each digital channel, clearly showing channel independence between transaction initiation and verification
requirements.rationale: Flow documentation provides auditable evidence of channel independence and supports security reviews and BNM examinations

requirements.requirement: Review and update transaction verification thresholds at least annually based on fraud trend analysis, customer transaction patterns, and regulatory guidance
requirements.rationale: Static thresholds become outdated as fraud patterns and transaction values evolve; regular review ensures continued effectiveness

12.6.subsection: Digital Fraud Management and Customer Awareness
12.6.title: Customer Fraud Risk Empowerment

business.summary: Weak session management can enable session hijacking and unauthorised account access, directly threatening customer funds and the institution's reputation.

requirements.requirement: Define session timeout values appropriate to each digital channel and risk context, balancing security with customer usability across internet banking, mobile banking, and payment portals
requirements.rationale: Overly long sessions increase the risk of hijacking while overly short sessions frustrate customers; channel-appropriate timeouts balance both concerns

requirements.requirement: Provide customers with visibility of active sessions and the ability to terminate sessions remotely across all digital channels
requirements.rationale: Customer self-service session management reduces reliance on call centres and empowers customers to respond immediately to suspected compromise

technology.summary: Session management must enforce automatic timeouts, secure token generation, session fixation prevention, concurrent session controls, and proper token invalidation on logout.

requirements.requirement: Generate session tokens using cryptographically secure random number generators with sufficient entropy (minimum 128 bits) and transmit tokens only via secure HTTP-only cookies, never in URLs
requirements.rationale: RMiT explicitly prohibits session tokens in URLs; cryptographically strong tokens prevent prediction and brute-force attacks

requirements.requirement: Implement session fixation prevention by regenerating session identifiers upon authentication, privilege escalation, and any change in security context
requirements.rationale: Session fixation is a well-known attack vector where pre-set session IDs are used to hijack authenticated sessions

requirements.requirement: Enforce concurrent session controls limiting the number of simultaneous active sessions per user, with configurable policies for session displacement or blocking
requirements.rationale: Concurrent session controls prevent simultaneous exploitation from multiple locations if credentials are compromised

requirements.requirement: Implement server-side session invalidation on logout, idle timeout, and absolute timeout, ensuring all session state and associated tokens are completely destroyed
requirements.rationale: RMiT requires session invalidation upon logout; server-side enforcement prevents client-side bypass of session termination

governance.summary: Session management standards must formalise timeout values, token handling requirements, and concurrent session policies across all digital banking channels.

requirements.requirement: Develop and maintain Session Management Standards specifying timeout values, token generation requirements, session fixation mitigations, and concurrent session policies for each digital channel
requirements.rationale: Formal standards ensure consistent session security across all digital channels and provide the auditable baseline required by RMiT

requirements.requirement: Include session management security testing in the scope of all digital channel penetration tests, specifically verifying token security, timeout enforcement, and session fixation resistance
requirements.rationale: Targeted testing validates that session management controls work as designed and meet the standards specified in governance documents

requirements.requirement: Maintain configuration evidence showing current session timeout settings and concurrent session limits for all production digital channel environments
requirements.rationale: Configuration evidence demonstrates ongoing compliance and enables rapid validation during BNM examinations

12.7.subsection: Digital Fraud Management and Customer Awareness
12.7.title: Customer Fraud Awareness Programmes

business.summary: Real-time fraud detection is essential to protect customers and the institution from financial losses arising from increasingly sophisticated digital banking fraud schemes.

requirements.requirement: Establish fraud detection KPIs including detection rate, false positive rate, mean time to detect, and mean time to block, with quarterly reporting to senior management
requirements.rationale: Without measurable KPIs, the institution cannot demonstrate fraud detection effectiveness or identify degradation in system performance

requirements.requirement: Define and maintain fraud investigation and response procedures with clear escalation paths, customer notification requirements, and regulatory reporting obligations under BNM guidelines
requirements.rationale: Timely fraud response minimises customer losses and meets BNM's expectations for incident handling in digital banking services

requirements.requirement: Conduct quarterly fraud trend analysis using transaction data, customer complaints, and industry threat intelligence to inform rule and model updates
requirements.rationale: Fraud patterns evolve rapidly; regular trend analysis ensures detection systems remain effective against current attack methods

technology.summary: Real-time fraud detection systems must employ behavioural analytics, transaction pattern analysis, and anomaly detection with the capability to flag and block suspicious transactions.

requirements.requirement: Deploy a real-time fraud detection engine capable of evaluating transactions against rule-based and machine learning models within the transaction processing timeframe (sub-second latency)
requirements.rationale: RMiT requires real-time fraud detection; post-transaction analysis alone is insufficient to prevent fraudulent transfers

requirements.requirement: Implement behavioural analytics that profile customer transaction patterns, device usage, login times, and geographic locations to identify anomalous activities
requirements.rationale: Behavioural analytics is explicitly required by RMiT and provides context-aware detection that static rules cannot achieve

requirements.requirement: Configure automated transaction blocking or holding for transactions exceeding defined risk thresholds, with real-time alerts to the fraud operations team for review
requirements.rationale: The ability to block suspicious transactions in real time is the ultimate control preventing actual financial loss from fraud

requirements.requirement: Integrate fraud detection systems with transaction monitoring, anti-money laundering, and security event management platforms for comprehensive risk visibility
requirements.rationale: Integrated systems provide holistic fraud detection by correlating signals across multiple data sources and risk domains

governance.summary: Fraud detection governance must ensure detection rules and models are regularly reviewed, investigation procedures are formalised, and effectiveness metrics are reported to senior management.

requirements.requirement: Establish a Fraud Detection and Investigation Procedure covering alert triage, investigation workflows, customer communication, transaction reversal, and regulatory reporting
requirements.rationale: Formalised procedures ensure consistent and timely fraud response that meets both customer protection and regulatory requirements

requirements.requirement: Conduct quarterly reviews of fraud detection rules and models to assess effectiveness, tune thresholds, and incorporate new fraud patterns identified through trend analysis
requirements.rationale: Static rules degrade over time as fraudsters adapt; regular model review maintains detection effectiveness

requirements.requirement: Produce quarterly fraud detection effectiveness reports covering detection rates, false positive rates, blocked amounts, and comparison against industry benchmarks
requirements.rationale: Effectiveness reporting provides senior management and BNM examiners with evidence that fraud detection systems are performing as required

12.8.subsection: Digital Fraud Management and Customer Awareness
12.8.title: Customer Account Suspension and Kill Switch

business.summary: Digital customer onboarding introduces fraud and identity theft risks that must be managed through proportionate security controls without creating excessive friction for legitimate customers.

requirements.requirement: Ensure digital onboarding security controls are proportionate to the risk level of the products and services offered, with enhanced verification for higher-risk products such as credit facilities
requirements.rationale: RMiT requires controls commensurate with product risk; a savings account opening may warrant lighter controls than a credit card application

requirements.requirement: Track and report digital onboarding fraud rates, identity verification failure rates, and false rejection rates to assess the balance between security and customer experience
requirements.rationale: Monitoring onboarding metrics enables data-driven decisions about control calibration and identifies emerging fraud vectors

requirements.requirement: Maintain alignment with BNM e-KYC guidelines and Securities Commission requirements for digital onboarding of financial products
requirements.rationale: Digital onboarding must comply with multiple Malaysian regulatory frameworks beyond RMiT, including anti-money laundering requirements

technology.summary: Digital onboarding must implement identity verification, liveness detection, document verification, and fraud screening controls appropriate to the risk profile of the product being offered.

requirements.requirement: Implement automated identity verification integrating with MyKad/MyKAS validation, facial recognition comparison against identity document photos, and sanctions/PEP screening databases
requirements.rationale: Automated identity verification provides scalable and consistent checking that manual processes cannot achieve for digital onboarding

requirements.requirement: Deploy liveness detection technology that resists photo, video, and mask-based spoofing attacks during the facial verification step of digital onboarding
requirements.rationale: Liveness detection is explicitly mentioned in RMiT as a required control to prevent identity spoofing during digital onboarding

requirements.requirement: Implement document verification with OCR extraction, authenticity checks (watermarks, holograms, microprint patterns), and cross-referencing against submitted personal details
requirements.rationale: Document verification catches forged or tampered identity documents that could bypass facial recognition alone

governance.summary: Digital onboarding security standards should define verification requirements by product risk tier, establish fraud screening integration requirements, and ensure alignment with BNM e-KYC guidelines.

requirements.requirement: Develop and maintain Digital Onboarding Security Standards specifying identity verification, liveness detection, document verification, and fraud screening requirements by product risk tier
requirements.rationale: Formal standards ensure consistent onboarding security across all digital products and provide the governance framework expected by BNM

requirements.requirement: Conduct periodic assessments of digital onboarding security controls including testing of liveness detection bypass resistance and document forgery detection effectiveness
requirements.rationale: Regular testing validates that onboarding controls remain effective against evolving spoofing and forgery techniques

requirements.requirement: Maintain records of all digital onboarding verification steps and outcomes for each customer, supporting audit trail and regulatory examination requirements
requirements.rationale: Complete audit trails of verification decisions are essential for both fraud investigation and regulatory compliance demonstration

12.9.subsection: Digital Fraud Management and Customer Awareness
12.9.title: Post-Incident Customer Protection

business.summary: QR code payments and e-wallet services are rapidly growing in Malaysia and are vulnerable to tampering, overlay attacks, and forgery that can directly cause customer financial losses.

requirements.requirement: Ensure QR code payment flows display complete transaction details (merchant name, amount, reference) to the customer for confirmation before payment authorisation
requirements.rationale: Customer visibility of transaction details is essential to prevent QR code substitution and overlay attacks from succeeding

requirements.requirement: Establish merchant QR code registration and validation processes to prevent unauthorised or fraudulent QR codes from being used in the payment ecosystem
requirements.rationale: Merchant-side controls are essential because QR code attacks frequently target the generation and display of codes at merchant premises

requirements.requirement: Monitor QR payment transaction patterns for anomalies including unusual merchant volumes, geographic inconsistencies, and duplicate QR code usage indicative of tampering
requirements.rationale: Pattern-based monitoring catches QR fraud that individual transaction validation cannot detect

technology.summary: QR code and e-wallet systems must implement QR validation, transaction encryption, anti-tampering controls, and protection against overlay attacks to ensure transaction integrity and authenticity.

requirements.requirement: Implement cryptographic QR code validation that verifies the authenticity and integrity of QR codes before processing payment transactions, detecting any modification or substitution
requirements.rationale: QR code validation is explicitly required by RMiT to protect against tampering and forgery of payment QR codes

requirements.requirement: Encrypt all QR code transaction data end-to-end from the customer device to the payment processing backend using TLS 1.2+ and application-layer encryption
requirements.rationale: Transaction encryption is mandated by RMiT and prevents interception or modification of payment data in transit

requirements.requirement: Implement anti-overlay and anti-screen-capture protections in the e-wallet application to prevent malicious apps from intercepting QR code display or transaction confirmation screens
requirements.rationale: Overlay attacks are a specific threat called out in RMiT where malicious apps superimpose fake interfaces over legitimate payment screens

governance.summary: Formal QR code and e-wallet security standards must define validation requirements, encryption standards, and anti-tampering controls aligned with BNM and PayNet guidelines.

requirements.requirement: Develop and maintain QR Code and E-Wallet Security Standards covering QR validation, transaction encryption, anti-tampering controls, and alignment with PayNet DuitNow QR specifications
requirements.rationale: Formal standards ensure security requirements are clearly defined and consistently implemented across QR payment and e-wallet features

requirements.requirement: Conduct security testing of QR code payment and e-wallet features before each major release, including QR code tampering tests, overlay attack simulations, and encryption validation
requirements.rationale: Pre-release security testing validates that anti-tampering and encryption controls work as designed in real-world scenarios

requirements.requirement: Maintain alignment with DuitNow QR security specifications, PayNet guidelines, and Malaysian Payment Systems Act requirements for all QR-based payment implementations
requirements.rationale: QR payment security must meet multiple Malaysian regulatory and industry standards beyond RMiT alone

business.summary: A risk-based IT audit plan ensures that limited audit resources are directed at the technology domains posing the greatest risk to the institution's operations and regulatory compliance.

requirements.requirement: Ensure the annual IT audit plan covers all critical technology domains including cybersecurity, IT operations, change management, access control, business continuity, and digital banking services
requirements.rationale: RMiT mandates comprehensive coverage of critical technology domains; gaps in audit coverage leave significant risks unexamined

requirements.requirement: Align the IT audit plan with the institution's technology risk assessment, ensuring higher-risk domains receive more frequent and deeper audit coverage
requirements.rationale: A risk-based approach ensures audit effort is proportionate to actual risk rather than spread uniformly across all technology areas

requirements.requirement: Allocate sufficient budget and qualified IT audit resources to execute the approved audit plan within the planned timeline without compromising audit quality
requirements.rationale: Under-resourced audit plans lead to deferred audits and incomplete coverage, creating unacceptable risk blind spots

technology.summary: The IT audit plan must leverage appropriate audit tools and methodologies to effectively assess technical controls across cybersecurity, operations, change management, and access control domains.

requirements.requirement: Deploy and maintain IT audit management tools (e.g., TeamMate, AuditBoard) that support risk-based audit planning, work paper management, and finding tracking across all IT audit engagements
requirements.rationale: Audit management tools provide the structured workflow and documentation required for professional IT audit execution and regulatory evidence

requirements.requirement: Ensure IT auditors have access to appropriate technical testing tools (vulnerability scanners, log analysers, configuration audit tools) to conduct substantive technical assessments
requirements.rationale: Technical tools enable auditors to independently verify controls rather than relying solely on management representations

requirements.requirement: Maintain an IT audit universe inventory that maps all auditable technology entities, their risk ratings, last audit dates, and planned audit cycle frequencies
requirements.rationale: A complete audit universe ensures no critical technology domain is overlooked in the risk-based planning process

governance.summary: The IT audit plan must be approved by the audit committee and executed by qualified IT auditors, with formal risk-based scoping methodology documented and consistently applied.

requirements.requirement: Submit the annual IT audit plan to the audit committee for formal approval before the start of each audit cycle, including scope, methodology, resource allocation, and timeline
requirements.rationale: Audit committee approval is an explicit mandatory requirement under RMiT clause 13.1 and ensures board-level oversight of IT audit coverage

requirements.requirement: Document and maintain a risk-based audit scoping methodology that defines how technology risk assessments translate into audit priorities, scope, and frequency
requirements.rationale: A formal methodology ensures consistent, defensible audit planning decisions that can withstand regulatory scrutiny

requirements.requirement: Report quarterly to the audit committee on IT audit plan execution progress, highlighting any deviations from the approved plan and the rationale for changes
requirements.rationale: Regular progress reporting ensures the audit committee maintains oversight of audit execution and can address resource or scope issues promptly

business.summary: IT audit independence is fundamental to providing objective assurance to the board and BNM that technology risks are being effectively managed without conflicts of interest.

requirements.requirement: Ensure the internal audit function reports directly to the audit committee with no reporting line through CTO, CIO, or any IT management function
requirements.rationale: RMiT mandates direct audit committee reporting to prevent IT management from influencing audit scope, findings, or recommendations

requirements.requirement: Prohibit IT audit personnel from performing operational IT duties or participating in technology projects that they may subsequently be required to audit
requirements.rationale: Separation of audit and operational roles is fundamental to audit independence and objectivity

requirements.requirement: Ensure the audit committee reviews and approves the IT audit budget independently from the IT department's budget process
requirements.rationale: Financial independence prevents IT management from constraining audit resources to limit the scope of audit scrutiny

technology.summary: IT auditors must have unrestricted read-only access to all systems, logs, and records necessary for audit activities without requiring IT operational team approval.

requirements.requirement: Provision IT audit personnel with dedicated read-only access to all technology systems, databases, logs, and configuration repositories required for audit activities
requirements.rationale: RMiT requires unrestricted access to all systems and records; dedicated audit accounts ensure access is available without dependence on IT operations

requirements.requirement: Implement audit logging of auditor access to systems to maintain accountability while ensuring audit access cannot be restricted or monitored by the teams being audited
requirements.rationale: Audit trail of auditor access supports accountability without compromising independence from audited teams

requirements.requirement: Provide IT audit with direct access to security event management (SIEM) platforms, vulnerability management systems, and change management tools for independent verification
requirements.rationale: Direct tool access enables auditors to independently verify controls rather than relying on reports generated by the teams they are auditing

governance.summary: Formal policies must establish and protect IT audit independence, documenting reporting lines, access rights, and conflict-of-interest safeguards in the audit charter.

requirements.requirement: Establish an Audit Independence Policy that explicitly defines the IT audit function's independence from IT management, including reporting lines, access rights, and conflict-of-interest provisions
requirements.rationale: A formal policy provides the foundational governance document required to demonstrate audit independence to BNM examiners

requirements.requirement: Include IT audit independence provisions in the Audit Committee Charter, specifying the committee's responsibility for approving IT audit plans, budgets, and reviewing IT audit results directly
requirements.rationale: Charter-level provisions ensure audit independence has board-level backing and cannot be undermined by management

requirements.requirement: Conduct an annual independence self-assessment for all IT audit personnel, documenting any potential conflicts of interest and mitigating actions taken
requirements.rationale: Regular independence assessment identifies and addresses situations where auditor objectivity could be compromised

business.summary: IT audit quality depends on auditors possessing relevant certifications and current knowledge of technology risks, cybersecurity threats, and auditing standards.

requirements.requirement: Ensure IT audit team members hold relevant professional certifications such as CISA, CISSP, CRISC, or equivalent, with a target of 100% certification for senior IT auditors
requirements.rationale: Professional certifications demonstrate baseline competency and are expected by BNM examiners when evaluating IT audit capability

requirements.requirement: Allocate sufficient training budget to provide each IT auditor with a minimum of 40 hours of continuous professional development annually, covering emerging technology risks and audit methodologies
requirements.rationale: Technology evolves rapidly; auditors who do not maintain current knowledge cannot effectively assess emerging risks such as cloud security, API security, and AI-related controls

requirements.requirement: Supplement internal IT audit capability with external specialist auditors for highly technical domains such as penetration testing, cloud security, and blockchain where in-house expertise is limited
requirements.rationale: Not all specialist skills can be maintained in-house; engaging external experts ensures audit quality for technically complex areas

technology.summary: IT auditors must maintain hands-on technical proficiency with current audit tools, security testing methodologies, and the specific technologies deployed by the institution.

requirements.requirement: Provide IT auditors with hands-on training in the institution's core technology platforms including digital banking, cloud infrastructure, network security, and database systems
requirements.rationale: Auditors who understand the specific technologies in use can conduct more effective and relevant audits

requirements.requirement: Ensure IT auditors are trained in current cybersecurity threat landscapes, attack techniques, and defensive technologies relevant to the Malaysian financial services sector
requirements.rationale: Cybersecurity knowledge is essential for auditing the institution's security controls and assessing their effectiveness against real-world threats

requirements.requirement: Maintain IT audit team proficiency in computer-assisted audit techniques (CAATs) and data analytics tools for efficient analysis of large datasets during audit engagements
requirements.rationale: Data analytics capabilities enable auditors to perform comprehensive testing rather than relying on limited sampling approaches

governance.summary: A formal training and development plan must ensure IT auditors continuously develop their skills, with competency assessments tracking certification status and professional development progress.

requirements.requirement: Develop and maintain an annual IT Audit Training and Development Plan that identifies skill gaps, required certifications, and training activities for each audit team member
requirements.rationale: A structured training plan ensures professional development is planned, tracked, and aligned with the institution's audit needs

requirements.requirement: Conduct annual competency assessments for all IT audit personnel evaluating technical knowledge, audit methodology proficiency, and professional certification status
requirements.rationale: Competency assessments identify individual and team-wide skill gaps that need to be addressed through targeted training

requirements.requirement: Maintain a centralised register of IT auditor certifications, CPD hours, training completions, and competency assessment results for regulatory examination readiness
requirements.rationale: A complete competency register provides immediate evidence of audit team qualifications during BNM examinations

business.summary: Effective follow-up on IT audit findings ensures that identified risks are actually remediated rather than merely documented, protecting the institution from recurring technology failures.

requirements.requirement: Require management to provide documented action plans with specific owners, target dates, and milestones for all IT audit findings within 30 days of report issuance
requirements.rationale: Timely action plans demonstrate management accountability and are essential for the structured remediation process required by RMiT

requirements.requirement: Escalate overdue IT audit findings to senior management within 30 days of target date breach and to the audit committee if findings remain overdue beyond 60 days
requirements.rationale: RMiT mandates escalation of overdue findings to senior management and the audit committee to ensure accountability and timely resolution

requirements.requirement: Report the status of all open IT audit findings to the audit committee quarterly, including ageing analysis, overdue items, and trend analysis of finding categories
requirements.rationale: Regular reporting enables the audit committee to monitor remediation progress and intervene when management is not adequately addressing technology risks

technology.summary: Audit finding tracking systems must support action plan management, automated reminders, escalation workflows, and comprehensive reporting on finding remediation status.

requirements.requirement: Implement an audit finding tracking system (e.g., TeamMate, AuditBoard, or equivalent) that captures finding details, risk ratings, action plans, owners, target dates, and closure evidence
requirements.rationale: A dedicated tracking system ensures findings are systematically managed rather than lost in email or spreadsheet-based processes

requirements.requirement: Configure automated reminders and escalation workflows in the finding tracking system to alert action plan owners of approaching deadlines and automatically escalate overdue items
requirements.rationale: Automated escalation ensures overdue findings receive timely management attention without relying on manual follow-up

requirements.requirement: Generate automated dashboards and reports showing finding status by risk rating, age, owner, and technology domain to support audit committee reporting requirements
requirements.rationale: Automated reporting reduces manual effort and ensures consistent, accurate status information for governance reporting

governance.summary: Formal audit follow-up procedures must define action plan requirements, escalation timelines, closure verification processes, and audit committee reporting obligations.

requirements.requirement: Establish an Audit Follow-Up Procedure that defines action plan requirements, progress monitoring frequency, escalation timelines, and finding closure verification criteria
requirements.rationale: A formal procedure ensures consistent follow-up practices and provides the governance framework required to demonstrate compliance with RMiT

requirements.requirement: Require independent verification by internal audit that remediation actions have been effectively implemented before closing any IT audit finding rated as high or critical risk
requirements.rationale: Management self-certification of closure is insufficient for high-risk findings; independent verification ensures remediation is genuine and effective

requirements.requirement: Maintain a complete audit trail of all finding status changes, escalation events, management responses, and closure evidence in the finding tracking system
requirements.rationale: A comprehensive audit trail demonstrates the rigour of the follow-up process during BNM examinations and external audits

business.summary: Critical third-party service providers can introduce significant security and operational risks; independent security assessments are essential to verify that vendors meet the institution's security requirements.

requirements.requirement: Maintain a register of all critical third-party service providers with their risk classification, services provided, data access levels, and assessment schedule
requirements.rationale: A comprehensive vendor register is the foundation for managing third-party risk and ensuring all critical providers are assessed as required by RMiT

requirements.requirement: Obtain independent security assessments of all critical third-party service providers at least annually, conducted by qualified assessors independent of the service provider
requirements.rationale: Annual independent assessment is an explicit mandatory requirement under RMiT; vendor self-assessments alone are insufficient

requirements.requirement: Ensure the risk management function formally reviews all third-party security assessment results and documents risk acceptance decisions for any identified control gaps
requirements.rationale: RMiT requires risk management review of assessment results to ensure findings are properly evaluated and risks are formally accepted or mitigated

technology.summary: Third-party security assessments must evaluate service provider security controls, data protection practices, and technical compliance with contractual security requirements.

requirements.requirement: Define a standardised third-party security assessment scope covering infrastructure security, application security, data protection, access management, incident response, and business continuity controls
requirements.rationale: A standardised scope ensures consistent and comprehensive assessment of all critical vendors against the same security baseline

requirements.requirement: Require third-party assessments to include technical vulnerability scanning and penetration testing of vendor systems that process, store, or transmit the institution's data
requirements.rationale: Technical testing provides objective evidence of vendor security posture beyond policy and process reviews

requirements.requirement: Assess third-party data protection controls including encryption at rest and in transit, data segregation, data retention, and data destruction practices for the institution's data
requirements.rationale: Data entrusted to third parties must be protected to the same standard as data held within the institution's own infrastructure

governance.summary: A formal third-party assessment policy must define assessment frequency, scope, qualified assessor requirements, and the process for reviewing results and managing remediation.

requirements.requirement: Establish a Third-Party Security Assessment Policy defining assessment frequency (minimum annual for critical providers), scope requirements, qualified assessor criteria, and remediation management procedures
requirements.rationale: A formal policy provides the governance framework required by RMiT for systematic third-party security assurance

requirements.requirement: Include contractual clauses in all critical vendor agreements requiring cooperation with security assessments, provision of assessment reports, and remediation of identified findings within agreed timelines
requirements.rationale: Contractual requirements ensure vendors are legally obligated to support the assessment process and address findings

requirements.requirement: Track and follow up on third-party assessment findings through to remediation, with escalation to senior management for critical findings that remain unresolved beyond agreed timelines
requirements.rationale: Finding follow-up ensures identified third-party risks are actually mitigated rather than merely documented in assessment reports

business.summary: Requiring critical vendors to maintain recognised security certifications provides ongoing baseline assurance and reduces the institution's assessment burden for third-party risk management.

requirements.requirement: Require all critical third-party service providers to maintain recognised security certifications (ISO 27001, SOC 2 Type II, or equivalent) as a condition of contract engagement and renewal
requirements.rationale: Recognised certifications provide independent assurance of baseline security practices and are explicitly required by RMiT for critical vendors

requirements.requirement: Monitor the certification status of all critical third-party providers on an ongoing basis, with automated alerts for certifications approaching expiry within 90 days
requirements.rationale: RMiT requires ongoing certification monitoring; a lapsed certification may indicate deteriorating vendor security practices

requirements.requirement: Trigger an immediate risk reassessment of any critical vendor whose security certification lapses, with escalation to senior management and consideration of service continuity options
requirements.rationale: RMiT explicitly requires risk reassessment upon certification lapse; the institution must evaluate whether to continue the relationship with an uncertified provider

technology.summary: Vendor certification tracking must be systematically managed with automated monitoring, expiry alerts, and integration with the institution's vendor risk management processes.

requirements.requirement: Maintain a vendor certification tracking register that records certification type, certifying body, scope, issue date, expiry date, and any scope limitations for each critical vendor
requirements.rationale: A centralised register provides the single source of truth for vendor certification status required for ongoing monitoring and regulatory reporting

requirements.requirement: Implement automated certification expiry monitoring that alerts the vendor management team and risk function when vendor certifications are within 90 days of expiry
requirements.rationale: Automated monitoring ensures no certification expiry is missed and provides sufficient lead time for vendor engagement and contingency planning

requirements.requirement: Verify the authenticity of vendor-provided certifications by confirming with the certifying body or checking accredited registries before recording in the tracking register
requirements.rationale: Accepting unverified certifications creates a false sense of assurance; direct verification ensures certifications are genuine and current

governance.summary: A formal policy must mandate vendor certification requirements, define acceptable certifications, establish monitoring processes, and prescribe actions for certification lapses.

requirements.requirement: Establish a Third-Party Certification Policy defining required certifications by vendor criticality tier, acceptable certification standards, monitoring frequency, and lapse response procedures
requirements.rationale: A formal policy ensures consistent certification requirements across all vendor relationships and provides the governance framework required by RMiT

requirements.requirement: Include certification maintenance requirements in all critical vendor contracts, specifying required certifications, notification obligations for certification changes, and consequences of certification lapse
requirements.rationale: Contractual obligations ensure vendors are legally bound to maintain certifications and notify the institution of any changes in certification status

requirements.requirement: Develop a certification lapse response procedure defining the risk reassessment process, interim controls, escalation paths, and criteria for continuing or terminating the vendor relationship
requirements.rationale: A predefined response procedure ensures the institution responds consistently and promptly to certification lapses rather than making ad hoc decisions

business.summary: A comprehensive security awareness programme reduces the institution's exposure to human-factor cyber risks by ensuring every individual with system access understands their security responsibilities.

requirements.requirement: Establish a mandatory annual security awareness training programme covering all employees, contractors, and third-party personnel with access to institutional systems
requirements.rationale: Human error remains the leading cause of security breaches; mandatory training ensures a baseline level of security knowledge across the entire workforce

requirements.requirement: Ensure training content addresses cybersecurity risks, data protection obligations under PDPA, and the institution's security policies and acceptable use standards
requirements.rationale: Employees must understand both technical threats and regulatory obligations to make informed decisions that protect the institution and its customers

requirements.requirement: Track and report training completion rates with a target of 95% or higher across all personnel categories, escalating non-completion to line management
requirements.rationale: Without completion tracking and enforcement, training programmes become optional and fail to deliver consistent risk reduction

technology.summary: Technology platforms must support scalable delivery, tracking, and reporting of security awareness training across all personnel categories including remote and third-party users.

requirements.requirement: Deploy a Learning Management System (LMS) capable of delivering security awareness modules, tracking completion, and generating compliance reports for all personnel categories
requirements.rationale: Manual tracking of training across employees, contractors, and third parties is error-prone and does not scale; an LMS provides automated compliance evidence

requirements.requirement: Implement automated reminders and escalation workflows for personnel who have not completed mandatory training within the specified timeframe
requirements.rationale: Automated nudges and escalation ensure timely completion without relying on manual follow-up processes

requirements.requirement: Integrate LMS training records with HR systems to ensure onboarding, role changes, and offboarding trigger appropriate training assignments
requirements.rationale: Integration prevents gaps where new joiners or role-changers miss mandatory training due to manual enrolment delays

governance.summary: Formal governance structures must ensure the security awareness programme is documented, reviewed, and continuously improved based on threat landscape changes and completion metrics.

requirements.requirement: Document and obtain board or senior management approval for the security awareness programme, including scope, objectives, target audience, and training calendar
requirements.rationale: Board-level endorsement demonstrates organisational commitment and ensures adequate resourcing for the programme

requirements.requirement: Conduct an annual review of training content to incorporate emerging threats, recent incidents, and regulatory updates relevant to the Malaysian financial sector
requirements.rationale: Static training content becomes outdated quickly; annual reviews ensure relevance to current threats such as ransomware, deepfakes, and evolving BNM requirements

requirements.requirement: Report training completion metrics and programme effectiveness to the board risk committee at least quarterly
requirements.rationale: Regular board reporting creates accountability and enables governance oversight of the institution's human-factor risk posture

business.summary: Regular phishing simulations provide measurable insight into the institution's human-factor vulnerability to social engineering, enabling targeted remediation before real attacks succeed.

requirements.requirement: Conduct phishing simulation exercises at least quarterly across all employees and contractors with email access to assess susceptibility to social engineering attacks
requirements.rationale: Quarterly simulations provide frequent data points to measure employee vigilance and detect deterioration before it is exploited by actual threat actors

requirements.requirement: Mandate follow-up training for all personnel who fail phishing simulations, to be completed within 10 business days of the simulation results
requirements.rationale: Targeted remedial training addresses individual knowledge gaps and reinforces correct behaviour while the simulation experience is still fresh

requirements.requirement: Track and report phishing simulation metrics including click rates, reporting rates, and repeat failure rates to senior management quarterly
requirements.rationale: Trend metrics enable management to assess whether the institution's security culture is improving and to justify investment in additional awareness initiatives

technology.summary: Phishing simulation platforms must support realistic scenario delivery, automated follow-up training assignment, and comprehensive metrics tracking across the entire organisation.

requirements.requirement: Deploy a phishing simulation platform capable of creating realistic social engineering scenarios including email phishing, spear phishing, and business email compromise simulations
requirements.rationale: Realistic simulations that mimic current attack techniques provide meaningful data on employee susceptibility to actual threats

requirements.requirement: Configure automated workflows to assign follow-up training modules to personnel who click on simulated phishing links or submit credentials
requirements.rationale: Automated assignment ensures consistent and timely remediation without relying on manual identification and follow-up processes

requirements.requirement: Integrate simulation results with the LMS and security incident reporting tools to enable comprehensive analytics and trend tracking
requirements.rationale: Integration provides a single view of human-factor risk metrics and enables correlation between simulation performance and actual incident data

governance.summary: Governance structures must define the phishing simulation programme scope, escalation procedures for repeat offenders, and reporting cadence to ensure sustained organisational commitment.

requirements.requirement: Document a phishing simulation procedure covering simulation types, frequency, target population, scenario design criteria, and follow-up training requirements
requirements.rationale: A documented procedure ensures consistent execution and prevents ad-hoc simulations that may not cover the full organisation or address relevant threat scenarios

requirements.requirement: Define escalation procedures for personnel who repeatedly fail phishing simulations, including progressive consequences and involvement of line management
requirements.rationale: Without escalation, repeat offenders represent a persistent vulnerability; progressive consequences reinforce the seriousness of security vigilance

requirements.requirement: Report phishing simulation results and trend analysis to the board risk committee quarterly, including benchmarking against industry norms
requirements.rationale: Board-level reporting ensures visibility of human-factor risk and enables informed decisions on security awareness investment

business.summary: Role-based security training ensures that personnel in specialised functions receive training tailored to their specific risk exposure and responsibilities, reducing the likelihood of role-specific security failures.

requirements.requirement: Provide specialised security training tailored to the specific risks and responsibilities of IT administrators, developers, security analysts, and senior management
requirements.rationale: Generic training is insufficient for personnel whose roles carry elevated security responsibilities; role-specific content addresses the unique threats and decision-making requirements of each function

requirements.requirement: Define role-based training curricula that map specific security competencies to each specialised role, with clear learning objectives and assessment criteria
requirements.rationale: Structured curricula ensure consistent knowledge delivery and enable measurement of competency attainment across different role categories

requirements.requirement: Update role-based training content at least annually to reflect emerging threats, new attack techniques, and technology changes relevant to each role
requirements.rationale: Threat landscapes evolve rapidly; outdated training content leaves specialised personnel unprepared for current attack vectors targeting their specific functions

technology.summary: Technology platforms must support differentiated training delivery and tracking by role category, enabling tailored content assignment and role-specific competency measurement.

requirements.requirement: Configure the LMS to support role-based training assignments that automatically assign relevant modules based on personnel role classification and department
requirements.rationale: Automated role-based assignment prevents manual errors and ensures every person in a specialised role receives their required training without delay

requirements.requirement: Provide access to specialised technical training platforms for developers (secure coding), IT administrators (hardening), and security analysts (threat detection and response)
requirements.rationale: Specialist roles require training depth beyond what general awareness platforms offer; dedicated technical platforms provide hands-on, scenario-based learning

requirements.requirement: Track and report role-based training completion and competency assessment results separately from general awareness training metrics
requirements.rationale: Separate tracking enables targeted reporting on specialised personnel readiness and identifies specific roles with training gaps

governance.summary: Governance must define which roles require specialised training, the competencies expected for each role, and the process for keeping role-based content current with evolving threats.

requirements.requirement: Document a role-based training programme that identifies all specialised roles requiring tailored training, maps security competencies to each role, and defines training frequency
requirements.rationale: A documented programme ensures systematic coverage of all high-risk roles and prevents ad-hoc or inconsistent training delivery

requirements.requirement: Establish a training content review cycle that incorporates threat intelligence, incident lessons learned, and technology changes into role-based training updates
requirements.rationale: Systematic content review ensures training remains relevant and incorporates real-world lessons from the institution's own incident experience

requirements.requirement: Report role-based training completion and competency metrics to senior management, highlighting gaps in specialised role readiness
requirements.rationale: Management visibility into specialised personnel readiness enables informed resourcing decisions and risk acceptance where gaps persist

16.1.subsection: Introduction or enhancement to digital services
16.1.title: Digital Services Notification Requirement

business.summary: Timely notification of material incidents to BNM is a mandatory regulatory obligation that protects systemic stability and demonstrates the institution's commitment to transparent regulatory engagement.

requirements.requirement: Notify Bank Negara Malaysia of all material technology and cybersecurity incidents within four hours of detection, including the nature of the incident, systems affected, estimated impact, and initial response actions
requirements.rationale: The four-hour notification window is a strict regulatory requirement; failure to meet it constitutes a compliance breach and may result in supervisory action by BNM

requirements.requirement: Submit a detailed follow-up report to BNM within fourteen days of the initial notification, covering root cause analysis, full impact assessment, remediation actions, and lessons learned
requirements.rationale: The 14-day follow-up report demonstrates thorough incident investigation and remediation planning, enabling BNM to assess systemic risk implications

requirements.requirement: Define clear materiality criteria for determining which incidents require BNM notification, covering customer impact, financial loss, data breach, service disruption duration, and reputational impact thresholds
requirements.rationale: Without defined materiality criteria, the institution risks either under-reporting (regulatory breach) or over-reporting (diluting the significance of notifications)

technology.summary: Technology systems must support rapid incident detection, automated escalation to regulatory affairs, and pre-populated notification workflows to meet the four-hour BNM notification deadline.

requirements.requirement: Implement automated incident escalation workflows that trigger BNM notification preparation immediately upon a material incident being classified, with countdown timers tracking the four-hour deadline
requirements.rationale: Manual escalation processes are too slow and error-prone to consistently meet the four-hour deadline; automated workflows ensure timely notification preparation begins immediately

requirements.requirement: Maintain pre-populated BNM notification templates within the incident management system that capture required fields including incident nature, affected systems, estimated impact, and initial response actions
requirements.rationale: Pre-populated templates reduce the time needed to compile notification details and ensure all required information fields are captured consistently

requirements.requirement: Configure incident management tools to automatically log all notification-related timestamps including detection time, classification time, notification preparation time, and submission time
requirements.rationale: Auditable timestamps provide evidence of compliance with the four-hour window and enable post-incident review of notification timeliness

governance.summary: Governance procedures must define the end-to-end BNM notification process with clear roles, escalation paths, and approval authorities to ensure timely and accurate regulatory reporting.

requirements.requirement: Document a BNM incident notification procedure defining materiality criteria, notification content requirements, approval authorities, submission channels, and the escalation path from incident detection to BNM submission
requirements.rationale: A documented procedure ensures all stakeholders understand their roles and the notification process can be executed consistently under the pressure of an active incident

requirements.requirement: Conduct tabletop exercises at least annually that simulate the end-to-end BNM incident notification process, testing the ability to meet the four-hour deadline
requirements.rationale: Tabletop exercises identify bottlenecks in the notification process before a real incident occurs, ensuring the four-hour deadline is achievable in practice

requirements.requirement: Maintain a register of all BNM incident notifications submitted, including timestamps, content summaries, follow-up report status, and any BNM feedback received
requirements.rationale: A centralised register provides an auditable record of regulatory notification compliance and facilitates tracking of follow-up report obligations

16.2.subsection: Introduction or enhancement to digital services
16.2.title: First-Time Digital Services Notification

business.summary: Advance notification of major technology projects to BNM enables regulatory oversight of changes that could significantly affect the institution's operations or customers, preventing surprises that undermine systemic stability.

requirements.requirement: Notify BNM at least three months prior to the planned implementation of any major technology project that may significantly impact operations or customers, including project scope, risk assessment, and implementation timeline
requirements.rationale: The three-month advance notification is a mandatory regulatory requirement; late notification may result in BNM directing the institution to delay implementation

requirements.requirement: Define clear criteria for identifying which technology projects qualify as major and require BNM notification, considering customer impact, operational significance, and risk profile
requirements.rationale: Without defined criteria, the institution risks either failing to notify BNM of significant projects or overwhelming the regulator with unnecessary notifications

requirements.requirement: Include a comprehensive risk assessment with each project notification covering technology risk, operational risk, customer impact, and implementation risk with mitigation strategies
requirements.rationale: BNM requires risk assessments to evaluate whether the institution has adequately considered the risks of major technology changes before implementation

technology.summary: Project management and governance tools must support identification of notifiable projects, track notification timelines, and ensure three-month advance notification compliance.

requirements.requirement: Integrate BNM notification checkpoints into the project management lifecycle so that major projects are flagged for regulatory notification at the project initiation or approval stage
requirements.rationale: Early identification in the project lifecycle ensures three-month advance notification is feasible; late identification risks missing the deadline

requirements.requirement: Maintain a centralised project portfolio register that flags all projects meeting major project criteria and tracks their BNM notification status and submission dates
requirements.rationale: A centralised register prevents projects from proceeding without required notification and provides an auditable trail of notification compliance

governance.summary: Governance processes must embed BNM notification requirements into project approval workflows to ensure no major project proceeds without timely regulatory notification.

requirements.requirement: Document a technology project notification procedure defining major project criteria, notification content requirements, approval authorities, and the three-month advance submission process
requirements.rationale: A documented procedure ensures consistent identification and notification of major projects across all business units and technology teams

requirements.requirement: Require BNM notification assessment as a mandatory gate in the project approval process, with Compliance Officer sign-off confirming whether notification is required
requirements.rationale: A mandatory gate prevents major projects from advancing to implementation without the required BNM notification being submitted

requirements.requirement: Maintain records of all BNM project notifications including submission dates, project implementation dates, BNM acknowledgements, and any BNM feedback or conditions received
requirements.rationale: Comprehensive records demonstrate compliance with the three-month advance requirement and provide audit evidence of regulatory engagement

16.3.subsection: Introduction or enhancement to digital services
16.3.title: Simplified Enhancement Notification

business.summary: Pre-notification of material technology outsourcing arrangements to BNM ensures regulatory visibility over third-party dependencies that could affect the institution's operational resilience and customer data protection.

requirements.requirement: Notify BNM prior to entering into any material outsourcing arrangement involving technology services, including the scope of outsourced services, risk assessment, and oversight mechanisms
requirements.rationale: Prior notification is mandatory; entering into material outsourcing arrangements without BNM notification constitutes a regulatory breach

requirements.requirement: Define materiality criteria for outsourcing arrangements that consider criticality of the technology service, customer data exposure, operational dependency, and substitutability of the provider
requirements.rationale: Clear materiality criteria ensure consistent identification of outsourcing arrangements requiring BNM notification and prevent unnotified material arrangements

requirements.requirement: Include comprehensive oversight mechanisms in each notification, detailing how the institution will monitor the outsourced service provider's performance, security posture, and compliance with contractual obligations
requirements.rationale: BNM expects institutions to demonstrate that outsourcing does not diminish their ability to manage technology risks and protect customer interests

technology.summary: Vendor management and procurement systems must flag material outsourcing arrangements for BNM notification and track notification status throughout the procurement lifecycle.

requirements.requirement: Integrate BNM notification checkpoints into the vendor onboarding and procurement workflow so that material technology outsourcing arrangements are flagged before contracts are signed
requirements.rationale: Integration into procurement workflows prevents contracts being executed before BNM notification is submitted, ensuring the prior notification requirement is met

requirements.requirement: Maintain a centralised outsourcing register that tracks all technology outsourcing arrangements, their materiality classification, BNM notification status, and oversight mechanisms in place
requirements.rationale: A centralised register provides a single view of all outsourcing dependencies and ensures notification compliance is tracked and auditable

governance.summary: Governance frameworks must ensure material outsourcing decisions include BNM notification as a mandatory prerequisite, with documented risk assessments and oversight arrangements.

requirements.requirement: Document an outsourcing notification procedure defining materiality criteria, notification content requirements, risk assessment standards, and the approval process for material outsourcing arrangements
requirements.rationale: A documented procedure ensures all business units and procurement teams understand when BNM notification is required and what information must be provided

requirements.requirement: Require Compliance Officer sign-off on the materiality assessment for all technology outsourcing arrangements before procurement can proceed
requirements.rationale: Compliance sign-off creates a mandatory control point that prevents material arrangements from being entered into without notification assessment

requirements.requirement: Report all material outsourcing arrangements and their BNM notification status to the board risk committee at least annually, including any BNM feedback or conditions
requirements.rationale: Board visibility ensures governance oversight of the institution's outsourcing risk profile and regulatory engagement posture

16.4.subsection: Introduction or enhancement to digital services
16.4.title: Full Enhancement Notification Requirements

business.summary: Pre-notification of cloud adoption for critical systems or customer data to BNM is mandatory, ensuring regulatory oversight of data residency, shared responsibility, and concentration risks inherent in cloud deployments.

requirements.requirement: Notify BNM prior to adopting cloud services for critical systems or systems that process, store, or transmit customer data, including the cloud risk assessment, data residency arrangements, and shared responsibility model
requirements.rationale: Prior notification is mandatory; deploying customer data or critical systems to the cloud without BNM notification constitutes a serious regulatory breach

requirements.requirement: Conduct and document a comprehensive cloud risk assessment for each cloud adoption covering data security, data residency, vendor lock-in, concentration risk, and regulatory compliance before BNM notification
requirements.rationale: BNM expects a substantive risk assessment that demonstrates the institution has fully evaluated cloud-specific risks before committing to cloud deployment

requirements.requirement: Document data residency arrangements confirming where customer data will be stored, processed, and backed up, including any cross-border data transfer implications under Malaysian data protection law
requirements.rationale: Data residency is a critical BNM concern; the institution must demonstrate awareness and control over where customer data physically resides

technology.summary: Cloud adoption workflows must include BNM notification checkpoints, and technical teams must document shared responsibility boundaries and data residency configurations for each cloud deployment.

requirements.requirement: Document the shared responsibility model for each cloud service provider clearly delineating security responsibilities between the institution and the cloud service provider across all service layers
requirements.rationale: The shared responsibility model is a mandatory component of the BNM notification and ensures the institution understands which security controls it must implement versus those managed by the provider

requirements.requirement: Configure and validate data residency controls within the cloud environment to ensure customer data remains within approved jurisdictions, with technical evidence of configuration
requirements.rationale: Technical validation of data residency configurations provides concrete evidence that data sovereignty commitments in the BNM notification are implemented in practice

requirements.requirement: Integrate BNM cloud notification requirements into the cloud service adoption workflow to prevent deployment of critical workloads or customer data to cloud before notification is submitted
requirements.rationale: Workflow integration creates a technical control point that prevents cloud deployment before regulatory notification, reducing the risk of non-compliance

governance.summary: Governance processes must ensure BNM cloud notification is a mandatory prerequisite for any cloud deployment involving critical systems or customer data, with documented risk assessments and data residency arrangements.

requirements.requirement: Document a cloud adoption notification procedure defining which cloud deployments require BNM notification, notification content requirements, and the approval process ensuring notification precedes deployment
requirements.rationale: A documented procedure ensures all cloud adoption decisions are assessed against BNM notification requirements before deployment proceeds

requirements.requirement: Require joint sign-off from the CRO, CISO, and Compliance Officer on all cloud adoption BNM notifications before submission, confirming adequacy of risk assessment, security controls, and data residency arrangements
requirements.rationale: Multi-stakeholder sign-off ensures the notification reflects comprehensive assessment across risk, security, and compliance dimensions

requirements.requirement: Maintain a register of all cloud adoption BNM notifications with submission dates, deployment dates, BNM acknowledgements, and any conditions or feedback received from BNM
requirements.rationale: A centralised register provides auditable evidence of compliance with the prior notification requirement and tracks regulatory engagement

16.5.subsection: Introduction or enhancement to digital services
16.5.title: External Assurance Competency

business.summary: Timely reporting of significant technology audit findings to BNM demonstrates the institution's transparency and enables the regulator to assess whether material control weaknesses are being addressed across the financial sector.

requirements.requirement: Report significant technology audit findings to BNM within thirty days of the audit report being finalised, covering findings that indicate material weaknesses in technology risk management, cybersecurity controls, or regulatory compliance
requirements.rationale: The thirty-day reporting deadline is a mandatory requirement; delayed reporting may indicate the institution is concealing material weaknesses from the regulator

requirements.requirement: Define clear criteria for identifying which audit findings qualify as significant and require BNM reporting, covering material control weaknesses, systemic vulnerabilities, and regulatory non-compliance
requirements.rationale: Defined criteria ensure consistent classification of significant findings and prevent both under-reporting and over-reporting to BNM

requirements.requirement: Include management response and remediation commitments in all significant finding reports submitted to BNM, with specific actions, owners, and target dates
requirements.rationale: BNM expects to see that significant findings are being actively addressed; reporting without remediation plans signals inadequate governance

technology.summary: Audit management systems must support identification, classification, and timely reporting of significant findings to BNM within the thirty-day deadline.

requirements.requirement: Configure the audit management system to flag findings that meet significant finding criteria and trigger BNM reporting workflows with thirty-day deadline tracking
requirements.rationale: Automated flagging and deadline tracking reduce the risk of significant findings being missed or reported late

requirements.requirement: Maintain an automated audit finding tracker that monitors remediation progress for all significant findings reported to BNM, with alerts for overdue remediation actions
requirements.rationale: BNM may follow up on reported findings; an automated tracker ensures the institution can demonstrate progress at any time

governance.summary: Governance processes must ensure significant audit findings are identified, classified, reported to BNM within thirty days, and tracked to remediation with board oversight.

requirements.requirement: Document an audit finding reporting procedure for BNM defining significant finding criteria, reporting content requirements, approval authorities, and the thirty-day submission workflow
requirements.rationale: A documented procedure ensures consistent and timely identification and reporting of significant findings across all audit engagements

requirements.requirement: Present all significant technology audit findings and their BNM reporting status to the board audit committee, including remediation progress and any BNM feedback
requirements.rationale: Board audit committee oversight ensures accountability for addressing significant findings and maintains transparency with governance bodies

requirements.requirement: Maintain a register of all significant audit findings reported to BNM with submission dates, finding summaries, management responses, remediation status, and BNM feedback
requirements.rationale: A centralised register provides an auditable trail of compliance with the thirty-day reporting requirement and facilitates tracking of remediation commitments

16.6.subsection: Introduction or enhancement to digital services
16.6.title: Document Provision to Bank

business.summary: Timely and comprehensive response to BNM regulatory examination findings demonstrates the institution's commitment to regulatory compliance and ensures supervisory concerns are addressed with concrete action plans.

requirements.requirement: Respond to all BNM regulatory examination findings within the timeframes specified by the Bank, with action plans developed for every finding including specific actions, owners, and target completion dates
requirements.rationale: Failure to respond within BNM-specified timeframes is a serious compliance breach that may trigger enhanced supervisory action or enforcement measures
